title: Interplay between connectivity and passivating agents in perovskite quantum dot networks
abstract: Different treatments (comprising polymeric encasement and different atmospheres) are applied to quantum dot solids in order to modify their defect landscape. The role of the latter in both, carrier recombination and stability, is unveiled.

title: Acceptor modification of diindolocarbazole embedded multiple-resonance emitters for efficient narrowband deep-blue OLEDs with CIE y ≤ 0.08 and alleviated efficiency roll-off
abstract: We designed and synthesized two efficient narrowband deep-blue emitters, pICz-PPO and pICz-2PPO, via an acceptor modification strategy to optimize the charge carrier mobility and thus addressed the efficiency roll-off issue.

title: From a simple push–pull dye to a broad-band absorbing and photoconducting material by assembly with CuBr
abstract: A novel broad-band-absorbing, NIR anti-Kasha, photoconducting coordination polymer (UDS-6) is rationally designed from CuBr and a simple push–pull dye. Its structural, photophysical, and electrochemical properties are investigated thoroughly.

title: Boosting the dielectric and electrical performance of perovskite materials by collaborative augmentation with reduced graphene oxide nanosheets for cutting-edge storage solutions
abstract: Enhanced dielectric and conductive properties of perovskite tuned by rGO.

title: Evaluation analysis of electromagnetic wave absorption for self-assembled 3D graphene skeleton-supported BN/SiC composites
abstract: A 3D-graphene/BN/SiC microwave absorber with a hierarchical structure is prepared, whose absorbing performance can be easily regulated by oxidizing in air.

title: Efficient additive-free FAPbI 3 perovskite solar cells achieved by promoting homogeneity
abstract: A compositionally pure FAPbI 3 perovskite absorber is achieved by regulating the pumping conditions of a low-pressure assisted synthesis approach, showing the pivotal role of homogeneity control in the enhanced photovoltaic performance and stability.

title: Photo-gain optimization in multilayer organic phototransistors by study of space-charge limited current
abstract: The photo-gain and sensitivity of solution processed multilayer organic phototransistors are optimized by analyzing the space-charge limited current behavior of the different photoactive layers embedded in hole-only and electron-only diodes.

title: Research on tin-copper bimetallic phosphide nanoparticles as anode for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The SnCuP/PC- X was prepared by one-step solvothermal phosphating method, in which the introduction of copper not only affected the morphology of SnCuP/C- X , promoted the electrical conductivity, but also inhibited the growth of tin particles.

title: Postsynthetic modification of a Zn-MOF to yield an efficient H-bond catalyst in the preparation of biologically active macromolecules
abstract: In this research, TMU-52-UR an efficient H-bond catalyst-based pillar-layered MOFs, was synthesized using the post-modification method and used in the preparation of biological active macromolecules.

title: Achieving low energy consuming bio-based piezoelectric nanogenerators via modulating the inner layer thickness for a highly sensitive pedometer
abstract: Enhancing the output performance of bio-based PENGs by the synergistic effect between the trapped electrons and the inter-squeezing between adjacent particles.

title: Ultrafast scintillation at room temperature achieved in CsPbCl 3 -based single crystals through Br over-doping
abstract: The realization of sub-nanosecond scintillation in CsPbCl 3 -based single crystals by Br over-doping shows great potential for their application in ultrafast imaging.

title: Na 7 [SbW 6 O 24 ]: a new type of turn-off luminescence humidity sensor based on a lanthanide-free polyoxometalate
abstract: The impact of dehydration/rehydration processes on the luminescence properties of the Na 7 [SbW 6 O 24 ]· n H 2 O ( n = 0, 4, 10, 16) lanthanide-free polyoxometalates is reported. Na 7 [SbW 6 O 24 ] is a new turn-off luminescence sensor for detecting humidity.

title: Enhanced near-infrared detection in organic phototransistors via optimized donor–acceptor single crystals
abstract: We present a rapid and highly sensitive near-infrared (NIR) organic crystal phototransistor, constructed from p-type copper phthalocyanine (CuPc) and n-type 3,4,9,10-perylene tetracarboxylic dianhydride (PTCDA).

title: Donor incomplete ionization and mobility enhancement in ultra-thin silicon-on-insulator films doped by phosphorus end-terminated polymers
abstract: The injection of P atoms is studied confining the same P dose in a progressively thinner silicon-on-insulator film. To account for the incomplete ionization observed in ultra-thin samples, the effect of Si/SiO 2 interfaces must be considered.

title: Power generation from n-type NbCo 1− x Ni x Sn and p-type NbFe 1− x Mn x Sb ternary half-Heusler compounds: from materials development to module fabrication
abstract: The thermoelectric figure of merit zT in n-type Ni-doped NbCoSn and p-type Mn-doped NbFeSb half-Heusler (HH) compounds was successfully improved using the 18 valence electron count concept, demonstrating reliable power generation in HH-based modules.

title: Gallium nitride for ultrasensitive thermo-active switching in terahertz metamaterial micro–nanophotonic devices
abstract: The remarkable conductivity change of GaN under the excitation of a thermal signal is exploited to demonstrate a hybrid GaN metamaterial device that exhibits sensitive switching of the metamaterial resonance within the terahertz spectrum.

title: Exploring the electronic and superior piezoelectric properties of two-dimensional PH-SiX materials for high-performance silicon-based devices
abstract: PH-SiZn exhibits a d 11 value of 63.148 pm V −1 , which is higher than that of 2H-MoS 2 by a factor of 17. The compressive strain leads to an enhancement of piezoelectricity, with PH-SiAl in an improvement of 369.2% in piezoelectricity at 4% strain.

title: f → f transition luminescence of Eu 2+ in barium–aluminum–borate oxyfluoride glass ceramics
abstract: A new type of transparent luminescent glass ceramics with the ability to generate the line emission of Eu 2+ ions were prepared. EPR was used to explore the mechanism of transformation of the optical transition of Eu 2+ from 4f 6 5d 1 →4f 7 to 4f 7 →4f 7 .

title: Kilogram-scale high-yield production of PbI 2 microcrystals for optimized photodetectors
abstract: In this work, PbI 2 -based photodetectors with symmetric Au–Au and asymmetric Au-graphene electrode structures are made. The asymmetric PbI 2 device shows 3–4.8 times higher photocurrent compared to the symmetric device under the same light intensities.

title: Room-temperature gas sensors based on low-dimensional nanomaterials
abstract: We provide a roadmap for room-temperature operable low-dimensional semiconductor-type gas sensors, along with recent trends in their application fields for a comprehensive overview.

title: A novel ratiometric optical fiber X-ray sensor based on NaGdF 4 :Tb/CaAl 2 O 4 :Eu/Nd composites
abstract: A ratiometric optical fiber X-ray sensor is developed to realize accurate dose rate detection in various environmental conditions. The maximum relative radiation rate detection sensitivity is up to 9.06% (nGy s −1 ) −1 .

title: Near-infrared circularly polarized organic room temperature phosphorescence based on a chiral host–guest doping strategy
abstract: Near-infrared circularly polarized organic room temperature phosphorescence is achieved by doping a naphthalimide derivative guest into a tartaric acid derivative host.

title: Green spectral simulative ceramic pigments with combined laser absorption and high-temperature stability
abstract: The inherent conflict between the near-infrared plateau of green plants and the absorption of a 1064 nm laser prevents developing green spectral simulative materials with effective laser-suppression properties.

title: Transfer-printing of patterned PEDOT:PSS structures for bendable, stretchable and biodegradable electronics
abstract: A novel technique to transfer-print variously treated PEDOT:PSS films onto stretchable substrates, including a patterning strategy and electrical and morphological analyses is presented to pave the way for advancements in stretchable electronics.

title: Enhanced comprehensive performance of carbon-based hole-transport-layer-free CsPbI 2 Br solar cells by a low-cost and stable long chain polymer
abstract: Hole-transport-layer (HTL)-free CsPbI 2 Br solar cells based on a carbon electrode have attracted widespread interest because they have a simple device configuration and good light and thermal stability, and moreover are independent of high-cost HTLs and precious metal electrodes.

title: Recent advances in lead-free halide perovskites: from synthesis to applications
abstract: The recent progress and development directions in lead-free perovskites are summarized from the perspective of device physics and materials science.

title: Highly stretchable coaxial P3HT electrospun fibers with enhanced reversibility
abstract: Highly stretchable and reversible electrospun fibers with a fracture strain of approximately 1000% were developed. The doped fibers maintain electrical conductivity up to 400% strain.

title: Perovskite nanocrystals passivated by aromatic phosphonic acid for high-performance light-emitting diodes
abstract: Here the authors presented BPA-passivated FAPbBr 3 NCs. The presence of –PO groups in the BPA plays a critical role in preventing superoxide formation, resulting in high-performance LEDs with an efficiency of 12.9% and a luminance of 29 280 cd m −2 .

title: Sulfur-dissolved high-entropy alloys with ultrawide-bandwidth electromagnetic-wave absorption properties synthesized via a mechanochemical process
abstract: Further enhancing the effective absorption bandwidth (EAB) and the harsh-environment stability of high-entropy alloy (HEA) electromagnetic-wave absorbing (EMA) materials is of great practical significance and broad application prospects.

title: Lanthanide hydrogels with tunable circularly polarized luminescence (CPL) via supramolecular chirality induction
abstract: “ Supramolecular chirality induction ” from cholate assembly, along with non-covalent sensitization of various lanthanide emission, provides an efficient induction of tunable CPL in lanthanide-based soft materials.

title: In situ firmly anchored polypyrrole on NiV-LDHs for efficient aqueous-system asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Polypyrrole (PPY) and nickel-based polyvalent hydrotalcite (NiV-LDHs) electrode materials have been extensively investigated and studied in the field of supercapacitor electrodes.

title: A DFT study of the mechanism of NH 3 -SCR NOx reduction over Mn-doped and Mn–Ti co-doped CoAl 2 O 4 catalysts
abstract: This study reveals that manganese-doped and manganese-titanium co-doped catalysts enhance the catalytic performance of cobalt-chromium spinel NH 3 -SCR, leading to improved air quality and reduced emissions.

title: A self-healing and deformation responsive supercapacitor fabricated using a smart gel polymer electrolyte and a redox-activated electrode
abstract: A flexible intelligent supercapacitor is fabricated using a smart gel polymer electrolyte (PVA–H3BO3–H2SO4–MB–Na2MoO4) and a redox-activated electrode to achieve superior pseudocapacitance, self-healing and deformation responsive properties.

title: Recent advances in small-molecule organic fluorescent semiconductors
abstract: Recent advances in small-molecule organic fluorescent semiconductors are summarized from the aspects of aggregation structures, materials and properties, and their related optoelectronic applications.

title: Vertical organic electrochemical transistor platforms for efficient electropolymerization of thiophene based oligomers
abstract: Vertical organic electrochemical transistor platforms enable facile channel formation by electropolymerization. The improved deposition control and resulting high performance is demonstrated here with the trimer ETE-COONa.

title: Noise suppression of 0D/2D flexible photodetectors mediated with atomically thin F–C functionalization on graphene channel
abstract: Incorporation of carbon quantum dots with fluorine-functionalized graphene with atomically thin F–C functionalization is revealed to feature the merits of transparent and flexible UV photodetectors.

title: Improving the photovoltaic performance of perovskite solar cells through the molecular design of donor–acceptor polymeric hole-transport materials
abstract: The control of backbone geometry is found to be important for reaching enhanced charge-transport characteristics of newly designed polymeric HTMs. Encouraging efficiency of 19.1% with good stability for p–i–n PSCs is achieved.

title: Electrodeposition of 2D layered tungsten diselenide thin films using a single source precursor
abstract: We report the electrodeposition of WSe 2 thin films using a single source precursor (WSeCl 4 ) and its 2D anisotropic growth from a microfabricated TiN nanoband electrode across an insulator, allowing direct electrical characterization.

title: Flexible chalcogenide perovskite Ba 3 Te 2 S 7 with high electron mobility and strong optical absorption ability
abstract: The narrow bandgap, high electron mobility, significant optical absorption ability and favorable conduction-band offsets position flexible chalcogenide perovskite Ba 3 Te 2 S 7 as a promising bottom-cell material in all-perovskite tandem solar cells.

title: A dual-emission fluorescence coordination polymer for simultaneous quantification of Al 3+ and Pb 2+ in their mixtures
abstract: The compound 1 with dual emission bands exhibits different response signals to Al 3+ and Pb 2+ , allowing for separate and simultaneous detection of them. This study establishes a powerful platform for simultaneously quantifying multiple analytes.

title: Tunability of transport properties in semi-exfoliated Ag x CoO 2− δ (0.4 < x < 1) crystals
abstract: Delafossite silver cobaltites show a remarkable range of silver content in materials made by ion-exchange on single-crystal precursors. The results are partly exfoliated materials with charge carrier densities that vary by an order of magnitude.

title: Heat capacity and structural transition effect in polycrystalline kesterite
abstract: The importance of the heat capacity measurement is reported to estimate and analyse accurately the structural and thermal transport behaviour in kesterite-Cu 2 ZnSnS 4 .

title: Slow magnetic relaxation and luminescence properties in β-diketonate lanthanide( iii ) complexes. Preparation of Eu( iii ) and Yb( iii ) OLED devices
abstract: Six β-diketonate compounds [Ln(btfa) 3 (4,4′-dinonylbipy)] Ln = Sm, Eu, Tb, Dy, Er and Yb are reported. Magnetic and Luminescence study is also filed. The Eu and Yb complexes were successfully used as emitters in multilayer vacuum-deposited OLEDs.

title: Spectroscopic properties of yellow and red InP/ZnSe/ZnS quantum dots
abstract: The spectroscopic properties of yellow and red InP/ZnSe/ZnS quantum dots (QDs) were comparatively investigated. The red QDs exhibit a less serious Auger effect, a longer hot carrier cooling time and stronger multiphoton absorption cross-sections.

title: Highly efficient and thermally stable broadband green-emitting BaY 2 Sc 2 Al 2 SiO 12 :Ce 3+ phosphors enabling warm-white LEDs with high luminous efficacy and high color rendering index
abstract: A broadband green-emitting BaY 2 Sc 2 Al 2 SiO 12 :Ce 3+ garnet phosphor enables warm-white LEDs with high R a of 93.3 and high LE of 105.3 lm W −1 .

title: An eco-friendly wood sponge-based multifunctional pressure and temperature sensor for electronic skin
abstract: This work prepared a reduced graphene oxide coated wood sponge for high-property pressure and temperature multifunctional sensors which can realize high sensitivity, large pressure range, and low detection limit.

title: Near stoichiometric lithium niobate crystal with dramatically enhanced piezoelectric performance for high-temperature acceleration sensing
abstract: The NSLN, characterized by a lower density of V−Li defects and greater distortion of the NbO 6 octahedron compared to CLN, demonstrated superior piezoelectric performance and remarkable sensing capabilities.

title: Metallopolymer-based block copolymers for perfluorinated substances (PFAS) and ion removal
abstract: Porous metallopolymer membranes are prepared via the self-assembly and non-solvent-induced phase separation (SNIPS) process. These membranes efficiently remove (transition)metal ions and perfluorinated organic substances from the water phase.

title: Magnetic anisotropy evolution with Fe content in electrodeposited Ni 100− x Fe x thin films
abstract: Agreement between experimental results and a theoretical model. Crystalline structure, elasticity, shape and pairs contribute to the magnetic anisotropy of Ni 100− x Fe x alloys.

title: Temperature-stable Li 4 Ti 3 O 8 composite microwave dielectric ceramics and their applications in dielectric resonator antennas
abstract: Li 4 Ti 3 O 8 ceramics with low loss and high stability were prepared using the solid-phase method. The prepared DRAs have a high radiation efficiency (>90%) and a gain of 4.5 dBi, meeting the requirements for 5G/6G communication applications.

title: Aerosol-assisted chemical vapor deposition of 2H-WS 2 from single-source tungsten dithiolene precursors
abstract: Tungsten dithiolene complexes were used as single-source precursors for the aerosol-assisted chemical vapor deposition of WS 2 thin films.

title: Co-doping of Sb 3+ /Ln 3+ in the Cs 2 NaGdCl 6 perovskite leads to efficient energy transfer and single-host white light emission
abstract: Sb 3+ /Ln 3+ co-doped Cs 2 NaGdCl 6 double perovskite was prepared. The energy transfer relationship between Sb 3+ and Tb 3+ was explored. White phosphors with CIE coordinates of (0.34, 0.33) were obtained by introducing Ho 3+ into the Cs 2 NaGdCl 6 :Sb 3+ ,Tb 3+ .

title: Revealing the correlation relation between conducting channels in self-assembled monolayer tunnel junctions
abstract: With tunnelling induced light emission, correlation relations among multiple conducting channels in molecular monolayer tunnel junctions are revealed, which relates to the dynamic changes of interface contact or molecular conformation.

title: An integrated leather-based fluid transport wearable sweat device for electrolyte balance monitoring
abstract: The integrated leather-based fluid transport wearable sweat device achieves continuous and long-term monitoring of body physiology.

title: Disorder-to-order transition of regiorandom P3HT upon electrochemical doping
abstract: Upon repeated electrochemical doping and thermal annealing, regiorandom P3HT undergoes a disorder-to-order structural change.

title: Modulation of Auger recombination via facet engineering in CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: We demonstrate the modulation of Auger recombination via facet engineering in CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystals, where the static dielectric screening effect is revealed to prevail over the dynamic polaronic screening effect.

title: A patterning technology of transfer-free graphene for transparent electrodes of near-ultraviolet light-emitting diodes
abstract: A technology for the fabrication of transfer-free, patterned graphene on semiconductor or weakly catalytic metal substrate is presented, and the graphene transparent electrodes on GaN-based LED with 398 nm wavelength is fabricated accordingly.

title: Functionalized nickel oxide as a hole transport layer for organic solar cells with simultaneous enhancement of efficiency and stability
abstract: 2PACz-modified NiO x thin films successfully replace PEDOT:PSS as hole transport layers, and achieve a high PCE of 16.7% in the PM6:Y6-based organic solar cells with simultaneous enhancement of efficiency, stability and reproducibility.

title: Unravelling material properties of halide perovskites by combined microwave photoconductivity and time-resolved photoluminescence spectroscopy
abstract: Simultaneous measurements of trPL and microwave photoconductivity facilitate a global fit of charge carrier density in perovskite materials. This enables the extraction of fundamental rate constants and the mobility ratio of electrons and holes.

title: Phase-engineering compact and flexible CsPbBr 3 microcrystal films for robust X-ray detection
abstract: In this article, the introduction of a second phase with controlled defect states in the perovskite structure is shown to be advantageous for stable, high-energy X-ray detection.

title: Novel chemical recycling process of REBCO materials showcased on TSMG-processed waste
abstract: Due to the increasing production of rare-earth barium copper mixed oxide (REBCO) superconductors, a significant amount of REBCO waste is produced. The following recycling technique allows REBCO waste reprocessing into new precursor powder.

title: Evaluation of vanadium coordination compounds derived from simple acetic acid hydrazide as non-conventional semiconductors
abstract: Vanadium( v ) complexes prepared from acyl-hydrazones were obtained. The structural transformations were observed by applying in situ impedance spectroscopy, while electrical characteristics were correlated with thermal and structural properties.

title: Two-dimensional metal–organic nanosheets composed of single-molecule magnets: structural modulation and enhanced magnetism utilizing the steric hindrance effect
abstract: Tuning the bulkiness of the single-molecule magnet (SMM) alters the structures of SMM-nanosheets, which results in enhanced perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and SMM properties.

title: Synthesis and optical wireless communication application of high efficiency extreme blue CsPbBr 3 nanoplates
abstract: Extreme blue 435 nm emission, 97% PLQY CsPbBr 3 nanoplates and up to 150 Mbps optical wireless communication data transmission rate.

title: Correction: Orthogonal anthracene and pyrene derivatives for efficient pure deep-blue organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Correction for ‘Orthogonal anthracene and pyrene derivatives for efficient pure deep-blue organic light-emitting diodes’ by He Jiang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2023, 11 , 6438–6443, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TC00594A.

title: The HfO 2 ferroelectric–metal heterojunction and its emergent electrostatic potential: comparison with ZrO 2 and SiO 2
abstract: The emergent character of a cross-sectional cell containing two dissimilar HfO 2 –metal heterojunctions, where ferroelectricity and topologic transport are observed at room temperature in HfO 2 but are not expected in the monoclinic allotrope.

title: In situ transmission electron microscopy (TEM) study on the structural evolution behavior of nano Sn sheets under a thermal field
abstract: The pure Sn nanosheets were produced into nanospheres under the action of in-situ thermal field, and a heating experiment conducted on Sn and Cu interface samples, thereby elucidating the limitation of pure Sn as a solder material.

title: Combinatorial synthesis and characterization of thin film Al 1− x RE x N (RE = Pr 3+ and Tb 3+ ) heterostructural alloys
abstract: High-throughput combinatorial synthesis of Al 1− x RE x N (RE = Pr, Tb) thin films with 0 < x < 0.4 was performed to assess composition-phase-property relationships in an emerging materials family.

title: Solution-processed tungsten oxide with Ta 5+ doping enhances the hole selective transport for crystalline silicon solar cells
abstract: We demonstrate that low-cost, solution-processed WO 3− x films with Ta 5+ doping exhibit reduced oxygen vacancies and improved hole selectivity, achieving a remarkable PCE of 18.67% for WO 3− x /p-Si heterojunction solar cells.

title: Enhanced oxygen electrode performance in solid oxide fuel cells via La-doping of Pr 2 NiO 4+ δ -based Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites
abstract: Enhanced oxygen electrode performance in solid oxide fuel cells via La-doping of Pr 2 NiO 4+ δ -based Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites.

title: A self-powered flexible UV photodetector based on an individual ZnO-amorphous Ga 2 O 3 core–shell heterojunction microwire
abstract: The photoelectric performance of a ZnO-a-Ga 2 O 3 core–shell microwire can remain basically unchanged at different bending angles and bending cycles, indicating the potential application of the device in the field of flexible electronics.

title: Cold atmospheric plasma deposition of antibacterial polypyrrole–silver nanocomposites on wearable electronics for prolonged performance
abstract: This study demonstrates the use of Cold atmospheric plasma-assisted deposition of conductive PPy–Ag nanocomposite coatings onto fabric electrodes, for enhancing the long-term antibiofouling performance of e-textile-based electronics.

title: Dual narrowband photodetectors based on halide perovskite single crystal heterojunctions with solution-processed epitaxial growth
abstract: The figure illustrates the main device structure discussed in the paper, the incident direction of light, and the distribution of electric field intensity.

title: An organic photoelectrochemical transistor based on the Fe-MOF@PANI/carbon fiber for uric acid detection
abstract: The Fe-MOF@PANI/ carbon fiber gate material was prepared by solvothermal method and assembled into organic photophotochemical transistor (OPECT).

title: Biomedical applications of sensing devices with memristive behaviors
abstract: This review introduces the potential applications of biomimetic systems based on flexible memristive biosensors in the medical field of intelligent healthcare.

title: Liquid crystal elastomers in soft micro electromechanical systems: a review of recent developments
abstract: This review paper explores the potential of liquid crystal elastomers (LCEs) and their ionic counterparts (iLCEs) as versatile materials for soft micro electromechanical systems (MEMS), highlighting their growing interest within the field.

title: From energy to light: advancements in perovskite quantum dots for optoelectronics
abstract: Perovskite quantum dots (PQDs), with their distinct optoelectronic characteristics like their tunable bandgap and high photoluminescence quantum yield, have attracted a lot of attention as viable options for a variety of optoelectronic uses.

title: The impact of hydrogen peroxide production in OECTs for in vitro applications
abstract: Schematics of OECT with oxygen sensitive polymer (left) and air-stable polymer (right), showing electrochemical reaction with oxygen and cells cytotoxicity.

title: Fast synthesis of silver nanowires at room temperature via ultrasonication-mediated galvanic replacement for flexible transparent electrodes
abstract: In this work, we propose a facile method for fast synthesis of Ag nanowires by an ultrasonication-mediated galvanic replacement reaction between Ag + and raspberry-like Cu nanoparticles at room temperature.

title: Computational prediction and characterization of CuI-based ternary p-type transparent conductors
abstract: We report on the ab initio structure prediction of ternary Cu(S,Se)I alloys and characterise the discovered candidate systems for applications as p-type transparent conductors.

title: Metal ion-dependent structural phase transition and impact of the Jahn–Teller effect in two organic–inorganic hybrid compounds
abstract: Two new compounds, (H 2 dabco)[M(H 2 O) 6 ][SiF 6 ] 2 (dabco = 1,4-diazabicyclo [2.2.2] octane, M = Mn 2+ & Cu 2+ ), exhibit metal ion-dependent structural phase transitions, large dielectric response, SHG (M = Mn 2+ ) and ferroelasticity (M = Cu 2+ ).

title: 2D Ta 4 AlC 3 and Ta 4 C 3 nanosheets with excellent ultraviolet optical limiting behavior for laser protection
abstract: 2D Ta 4 AlC 3 and Ta 4 C 3 nanosheets exhibit excellent NLA properties, especially the strong OL effects, indicating that they are incomparable candidates for OL materials, which have great application prospects in the field of laser protection.

title: The structure of Pd-functionalized UiO-67 during CO 2 hydrogenation
abstract: Multi-technique operando investigation reveals the detailed evolution of UiO-67 MOF with embedded Pd nanoparticles under CO 2 hydrogenation reaction conditions at elevated pressures and temperatures.

title: Rare-earth chalcogenidotetrachloride clusters (RE3ECl4, RE = Dy, Gd, Y; E = S, Se, Te): syntheses and materials properties
abstract: We reported the synthesis and crystal structure of a series of rare-earth chalcogenidotetrachloride complexes. Comparative magnetic studies revealed fine but definitive differences due to using different metal and chalcogen elements.

title: Al 3+ substituted U-type hexaferrites Ba 4 Co 2 Fe 36− x Al x O 60 : structural, magnetic, electrical and dielectric properties
abstract: Polycrystalline samples of Al 3+ -substituted barium–cobalt U-type hexagonal ferrites, with the chemical composition Ba 4 Co 2 Fe 36− x Al x O 60 ( x = 0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8 and 1.0), were synthesised.

title: Three-dimensionally programmable soft crystals toward geometric phase photonics
abstract: Patterned monodomains of soft crystals enable programmable tuning in geometric phase photonics, showing prospects for application in flexible electronics and new displays.

title: Designing buried-gate InGaZnO transistors for high-yield and reliable switching characteristics
abstract: The study emphasizes the benefits of buried gate IGZO transistor devices, showcasing enhanced electrical performance and reliability.

title: Morphology evolution of core–shell carbon@mesoporous carbon microspheres with excellent microwave absorption performance
abstract: Tunable morphology core–shell carbon@mesoporous carbon microspheres were prepared for efficient microwave absorption.

title: Correction: Highly efficient and thermally stable broadband green-emitting BaY 2 Sc 2 Al 2 SiO 12 :Ce 3+ phosphors enabling warm-white LEDs with high luminous efficacy and high color rendering index
abstract: Correction for ‘Highly efficient and thermally stable broadband green-emitting BaY 2 Sc 2 Al 2 SiO 12 :Ce 3+ phosphors enabling warm-white LEDs with high luminous efficacy and high color rendering index’ by Xiaoyuan Chen et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2024, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4tc02906b.

title: Violet phosphorene as a saturable absorber for controllable soliton molecule generation in a mode-locked fiber laser
abstract: Controllable soliton molecules were generated in a Er-doped mode-locked fiber laser with a few-layered violet phosphorene saturable absorber.

title: Boosting the fill factor and open-circuit voltage of carbon-based perovskite solar cells with a graphene co-doped P3HT/NiO x hole-transporting bilayer
abstract: A low-temperature and solution-processed graphene co-doped G-P3HT/G-NiO x hole-transporting bilayer with enhanced conductivity and matched cascade band alignment is designed for high-performance carbon electrode perovskite solar cells.

title: Near room temperature thermoelectric performance improvement for Mg 2 Sn
abstract: Mg 2 Sn shows excellent thermoelectric properties at high temperatures. Alloying with Mg 2 Pb reduces the bandgap and lattice thermal conductivity. With further optimization of carrier concentration, the figure of merit is improved near room temperature.

title: A rationally designed singlet sink for glassy polymeric photon upconverting films
abstract: We synthesized a singlet sink to enhance upconversion (UC) quantum yield while maintaining a low intensity threshold in glassy polymer blends. This strategy enables higher sensitizer loadings and thus increases the scope of UC.

title: Metal–organic framework derivatives with gradient structures via multidimensional assembly engineering for tunable efficient microwave absorption
abstract: Multidimensional assembly strategy facilitates the preparation of MOF-derived Co-doped carbon-based composites assembled with 1D nanowires/rods and 0D nanobubbles to enhance and optimize microwave attenuation.

title: Theoretical evidence of the piezoelectric property enhancement for ScY- or CaTi-codoped wurtzite AlN
abstract: The higher piezoelectricity properties of (ScY) x Al 1− x N and (CaTi) x Al 1− x N alloys originate from the polarization enhancement of the materials caused by lattice distortion and charge redistribution.

title: Langmuir–Blodgett assisted alignment of 2D nanosheets in polymer nanocomposites for high-temperature dielectric energy storage applications
abstract: The Langmuir–Blodgett deposition technique enables a precise in-plane alignment and a densely packed arrangement of CNO nanosheets within the PEI matrix, resulting in a significant enhancement of energy storage performance at 150 °C.

title: Towards high-throughput exciton diffusion rate prediction in molecular organic semiconductors
abstract: Accurate property prediction is paramount to high-throughput screening of organic photovoltaics. Here, the accuracy of predicting exciton diffusion computationally is examined, and several simplifications towards high-throughput screening are explored.

title: Development of high-performance direct X-ray detector materials: from hybrid halide perovskites to all-inorganic lead-free perovskites
abstract: This review summaries the research progress of perovskite materials in X-ray detectors and provides ideas for the development of more environmentally friendly and higher-performance X-ray detectors.

title: Construction of a Zn 0.65 Cd 0.35 S/SnO 2 S-scheme heterojunction for efficient photocatalytic CO 2 reduction
abstract: A Zn 0.65 Cd 0.35 S/SnO 2 S-Scheme heterojunction was successfully constructed. The synergistic effect of the catalyst is improved by well-matched band edge positions. SnO 2 promotes photogenerated electron migration.

title: The new material science towards sustainable robotics
abstract: Bottom-up materials innovation enables sustainable soft robots with self-healing, degradation and recycling.

title: Amphiphilic acetylacetone-based carbon dots
abstract: The use of acetylacetone in combination with benzoic acid and ethylenediamine led to amphiphilic carbon dots formation with bright blue emission, which allowed us to produce LEDs, luminescent inks, and films for solar concentrators.

title: Computationally guided circularly polarized luminescence simulations in chiral adaptive supramolecular systems
abstract: Chirality and chiral phenomena associated with electromagnetic waves universally arise across many different forms, which involve transitions between states with opposite symmetries.

title: The effect of high dielectric BaTiO 3 nanoparticle dimensions on the dielectric properties and electro-optical performance of polymer dispersed liquid crystal films
abstract: Three different dimensional BaTiO 3 nanoparticles were synthesized and doped into PDLC films. By exploring and analyzing the mechanism, it was found that one-dimensional BaTiO 3 nanoparticles can effectively reduce the operating voltage.

title: Spinel CoFe 2 O 4 : a room temperature magnetic semiconductor with optical transparency
abstract: Finding a suitable ferromagnetic transparent semiconducting material is of utmost importance for the development of advanced devices with unique functionalities.

title: Effects of loosely bound electrons and electron–phonon interaction on the thermoelectric properties of electrenes
abstract: The interstitial charge in 2D HfI 2 exhibits high mobility due to weak lattice perturbation, and the high mobility induces a high power factor and ZT value.

title: Spin-state dependent pressure responsiveness of Fe( ii )-based triazolate metal–organic frameworks
abstract: We applied Fe( ii )-based MOFs with different spin-states to investigate the impact of Fe( ii ) spin-state on the bulk modulus. The results emphasize the complex parameter space that determines the mechanical properties of MOFs.

title: Hydrogen-bonded polyazomethines for efficient organic solar cells
abstract: Hydrogen-bonded polyazomethines were synthesized for organic solar cell devices.

title: Unveiling the dynamics of phase-transition from ferroelectric to relaxor behavior in Nd-doped BNT-based lead-free piezoelectric ceramics
abstract: Nd-doped BNKBT ceramics show a ferroelectric-to-relaxor transition and high piezoelectric performance, demonstrating a balance between ferroelectric and relaxor phases for improved electrical and structural properties in lead-free applications.

title: Lattice dynamics in chiral tellurium by linear and circularly polarized Raman spectroscopy: crystal orientation and handedness
abstract: Linearly and circularly polarized Raman spectroscopy allows to identify crystallographic faces, chiral axis orientation and crystal handedness of chiral tellurium, interesting toward the development of multifunctional devices.

title: Laser-induced graphene: synthesis advances, structural tailoring, enhanced properties, and sensing applications
abstract: This review explores laser-induced graphene (LIG), highlighting parameters for synthesis and microstructure tuning to enhance its properties for electrochemical and physical sensing applications, as well as its challenges.

title: A gas-phase alkali-halide-assisted stable precursor supplied from zirconium carbide for the synthesis of 2D large-sized ZrS 2 nanosheets
abstract: Large-sized lateral ZrS 2 nanosheets up to 22 μm were synthesized using a gas-phase alkali halide precursor, showing high crystallinity and a uniform surface and exhibiting field effect transistors behaviour.

title: Correction: Magnetic response of photonic crystals based on nucleating agents of binuclear complexes
abstract: Correction for ‘Magnetic response of photonic crystals based on nucleating agents of binuclear complexes’ by Mengdong Tu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2023, 11 , 16922–16927, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TC02786D.

title: Metal halide perovskite nanocrystals for plant-growth lighting applications: strategies for boosting stability
abstract: This review article provides a comprehensive overview of the advantages and latest progress of MHP materials for plant lighting. Efforts to enhance stability for plant lighting applications are explored.

title: An acceptor-quantity modulation strategy for high-performance ambipolar semiconducting polymers: from a dual-acceptor to a quad-acceptor backbone
abstract: The triple-acceptor P2DPP-BT shows a deeper LUMO level and a stronger π–π interaction, leading to the highest electron mobility of 1.1 cm 2 V −1 s −1 in organic transistors.

title: Tailoring the energy gap to promote long wavelength emission of nitrogen-doped sulfur quantum dots via dual functional ethylenediamine
abstract: Long wavelength emission nitrogen doped sulfur quantum dots were constructed by employing bifunctional ethylenediamine as a solvent and dopant, which is verified by the reduced energy gap based on Gaussian calculations.

title: Cluster-induced aggregation in polyurethane derivatives with multicolour emission and ultra-long organic room temperature phosphorescence
abstract: Cluster-induced aggregation, multicolour luminescence and ultra-long phosphorescence (0.45 s) in NCLPs based on polyurethane derivatives have been achieved by simply adjusting reaction temperature and reaction time; this is the longest reported phosphorescence lifetime for pure NCLPs.

title: Enhancing visual luminescence detection of tetracycline antibiotics through regulation of nitrogen-containing ligand and antenna effect of Ln 3+
abstract: Two water-stable luminescent Cd-MOFs were synthesized from flexible carboxylate ligand and two N-containing ligands with different rigidity and flexibility, which demonstrated different luminescence responses toward antibiotics TC/OTC.

title: Tunable light-induced topological edge states in strain engineering of bismuthene monolayers
abstract: Considering the combination of strain effects and external periodic light fields allows tuning of the nontrivial edge states in nanoribbons of bismuthene monolayers, i.e. , Bi(110) and Bi(111) monolayers.

title: Weakened charge trapping at the electrode/active layer interface in a bulk heterojunction-based organic phototransistor for quick photomultiplication
abstract: Incorporation of receptors leads to degradation of photodetection performance when metal electrode devices with more matched energy levels are used.

title: Template-free molten salt synthesis of pure and Sr-doped LaCrO 3 1D nanorods with enhanced electrical transport properties
abstract: Morphology control & doping are advantageous in material science. A new template-free molten salt synthesis route is developed for the synthesis of pure & Sr 2+ doped LaCrO 3 nanorods with high electrical conductivity, which are otherwise inaccessible.

title: Importance of the curvature in electronic, structural and charge transport properties: oligomers of N -pyridine carbazole
abstract: The recent development of π-conjugated nanohoops in organic electronics has opened new perspectives for this family of curved materials.

title: Lateral homojunctions of WSe 2 through contact surface engineering with SF 6 plasma etching
abstract: SF 6 plasma etching was developed as a contact surface pre-treatment to achieve efficient n-doping in multilayer WSe 2 nanosheets and build lateral homojunctions exhibiting superb photovoltaic properties and self-powered wide-spectrum photodetection.

title: High-efficiency organic light-emitting diodes based on cationic iridium( iii ) complexes with double tridentate ligands
abstract: Record-high device performance has been achieved with EQE max over 17% by cationic tridentate Ir( iii ) complexes.

title: Exploring the role of viologen and iodocuprate in the enhanced resistive switching performance of Anderson polyoxometalate-based three-component hybrids
abstract: The introduction of MV 2+ into Anderson-type (MnMo 6 O 18 L 2 ) 3− can trigger the resistive switching (RS) performance, and the further grafting of electron-rich Cu 2 I 3 gives rise to enhanced RS behavior with high thermal tolerance and long-term stability.

title: Exceptional thermal stability of lanthanide-phosphonate frameworks
abstract: A remarkable thermally-robust and stable metal–organic framework with lanthanide cations is obtained by the in situ formation of a polymeric phosphonate-based organic ligand.

title: Ultrahigh-power-density BNT ferroelectric multilayer ceramic capacitors for pulse power energy conversion components
abstract: Bi 0.5 Na 0.5 TiO 3 –BiAlO 3 –NaNbO 3 multilayer ceramic capacitors were prepared as pulse power energy conversion components. An ultrahigh output power density up to 2.2 × 10 9 W kg −1 (GW kg −1 ) with a output voltage up to 9.8 kV mm −1 was achieved.

title: A review on MXene-based microwave absorption composites: engineering, component optimization and structure regulation
abstract: This paper classifies MXene-based materials by composition and structure, showing that multicomponent designs and structural modulation enhance absorption. The paper also discusses future directions for MXene MA composites.

title: Unusual red luminescence and super thermal stability of a new narrow-band emission phosphor for backlight display application
abstract: This work reports the design and successful synthesis of an Eu 2+ -doped narrow-band red-emitting borate phosphor, and proposes a defect engineering approach for enhancing the thermal quenching resistance of a phosphor.

title: A six-level ferroelectric storage cell based on a bidirectional imprint field
abstract: In this paper, we propose and experimentally demonstrate a six-level ferroelectric cell with six switchable polarization states using a novel fixed-charge method.

title: Bimetallic CoNi-metal–organic framework@molybdenum disulfide core–shell Z-scheme heterojunctions with broad-spectrum response toward optimized photocatalytic performance
abstract: The CoNi-MOF@MoS 2 Z-scheme heterojunction photocatalyst with core–shell structure is prepared by a two-step hydrothermal method. The many active sites of MoS 2 and the high porosity of CoNi-MOF are conducive to the degradation of pollutants.

title: Advanced thermal/environmental barrier coatings of high-entropy rare earth disilicates tuned by strong anharmonicity of Eu 2 Si 2 O 7
abstract: Computational discovery and comprehensive property evaluation of advanced thermal/environmental barrier coatings of high-entropy rare earth disilicates alloys tuned by strong anharmonicity of Eu 2 Si 2 O 7 .

title: Structural, dielectric, and conduction behaviour of A-site deficient Sr x Na 1−2 x NbO 3 ceramics
abstract: The structural and electrical properties of A-site deficient Sr x Na 1−2 x NbO 3 (0 ≤ x ≤ 0.25) ceramics have been studied by X-ray diffraction, electron microscopy, dielectric spectroscopy, impedance spectroscopy and polarisation against electric field.

title: Low-temperature open-atmosphere growth of WO 3 thin films with tunable and high-performance photoresponse
abstract: In-situ orientation tuning of WO 3 thin films with high crystallinity is achieved with a low-temperature and open-atmosphere deposition technique. The fabricated devices showed a significant change in photo-response for film orientation.

title: Interfacial charge doping effect in C8-DNTT/PDIF-CN 2 heterojunction field-effect transistors
abstract: In C8-DNTT/PDIF-CN2 heterojunctions, the formation of an interfacial charge accumulation region provides a shift of the threshold voltage values and an improved resilience to the bias stress effect in comparison with single-layer devices.

title: Efficient and stable photocatalytic degradation of PET waste textiles through Ni 2 P–Cu 3 P/TiO 2 /C NFs facilitated by the synergistic effect of CTAB and p–n heterojunctions
abstract: A photocatalyst and CTAB synergistically catalyze the degradation of PET waste textiles under visible light.

title: Quinone-amine polymers prepared by simple precipitation polymerization and used as cathodes for aqueous zinc-ion batteries and electrochromic materials
abstract: Quinone amine polymers with multiple active sites exhibit excellent performance in aqueous zinc ion batteries and as electrochromic materials.

title: A broadband near-infrared emission Na 3 Al 2 (PO 4 ) 2 F 3 :Cr 3+ phosphor exhibiting zero photoluminescence quenching at 398 K
abstract: A novel Cr 3+ -doped phosphor is synthesized towards zero thermal quenching and broadband emission for NIR applications.

title: Efficient stepwise carrier concentration optimization in Ge (1+ x )− y Sb y Te
abstract: Owing to superior thermoelectric properties, GeTe shows practical applications in power generation and refrigeration.

title: Metal cation detection based on a stable n-channel accumulation organic electrochemical transistor
abstract: An n-channel high-performance, stable OECT device by using a highly crystalline fullerene derivative film as the channel layer has been prepared and has demonstrated promising potential for metal cation detection.

title: Investigation of potassium doping and defect healing mechanism in core–shell CsPbBr 3 /SiO 2 quantum dots
abstract: In this work, we firstly succeed in revealing the exact K + doping and defect healing mechanism in core–shell CsPbBr 3 /SiO 2 QDs.

title: High-performance organic thin-film transistors: principles and strategies
abstract: Organic thin film transistors (OTFTs) mark a breakthrough in flexible electronics, offering advantages over traditional inorganic semiconductors through their adaptability, cost efficiency, and low-temperature production.

title: Efficient and easily repeatable organic solar cells in a high boiling point solvent by introducing a highly mixed tolerant guest acceptor
abstract: Efficient and easily repeatable organic solar cells were fabricated based on a highly mixed tolerant acceptor in a high boiling point solvent.

title: Intermolecular lithium bonding between different components upon mixing simultaneously enhances the thermal and electrical properties of an amorphous organic semiconductor material
abstract: Simultaneous improvement in the thermal stability and electrical properties of an electron transport material by mixing components that form intermolecular lithium bonding between different mixing components.

title: Improved cycling stability and ON/OFF ratio of SrFeO x topological phase transition memristors using a La 0.7 Sr 0.3 MnO 3 bottom electrode
abstract: Single-crystal BM-SFO films with horizontal superlattice-like stripes were epitaxially grown on LSMO bottom electrodes and Au/SFO/LSMO memristors exhibit excellent I – V cycling stability and improved ON/OFF ratio.

title: Homogeneous bulk heterojunction domains with continuous charge pathway via hydrophobic membrane filtration for stable photo and dark current operations
abstract: Hydrophobic membrane filtration addresses solubility issues related to solvent and molecular weight. Organic optoelectronics with filtration exhibit stable operation under varying light intensities, reducing leakage current and improving charge flow.

title: A nanoflower-like GaSe/β-Ga 2 O 3 based heterostructure for highly efficient self-powered broadband photodetectors
abstract: The combination of GaSe nanoflowers with β-Ga 2 O 3 results in a high-performance heterojunction photodetector with self-powered operation and outstanding responsivity from deep UV to visible wavelengths.

title: Tungsten-doped barium stannate as a transparent conducting film
abstract: Epitaxial W-doped BaSnO 3 (on Sn site) thin films with promising Vis-NIR transparency and comparable carrier mobility have been fabricated on SrTiO 3 (100) substrate, implying its potential as a candidate for Vis-NIR transparent conducting oxide.

title: Stimuli responsive dynamic polymer networks with tunable toughness and elasticity through the regulation of covalent crosslinking and reversible hydrogen bonds
abstract: Construction of stimulus responsive dynamic polymer networks with tunable mechanical properties by regulating the covalent crosslinking and hydrogen bonds.

title: Metal coordination-induced time-dependent phosphorescence colors (TDPC) in multi-component-doped films for multifunctional applications
abstract: The time-dependent phosphorescence colors (TDPC) towards anti-Kasha emission from the T 2 energy level was activated by coordination effects in the multi-component system TPy-BA/ZnCl 2 /PVA.

title: Enhanced Curie temperature near 300 K in highly crystalline GdO epitaxial thin films concomitant with an anomalous Hall effect
abstract: The Curie temperature of a ferromagnetic semiconductor GdO was enhanced to 303 K due to the improved crystallinity and electron mobility.

title: Pressure-enhanced optoelectronic properties in the two-dimensional metal phosphorus trichalcogenide semiconductor SnPSe 3
abstract: A significant enhancement of the optoelectronic properties of SnPSe 3 was achieved by pressure, which will stimulate further pursuit of pressure-induced fascinating properties in two-dimensional semiconductors.

title: A two-dimensional lead-free double perovskite for self-powered and high performance X-ray detection
abstract: Two-dimensional Dion–Jacobson (DJ) phase hybrid perovskites are gradually coming to the stage of optoelectronic devices because of their excellent photoelectric properties.

title: Engineering flaky carbonyl iron/MoS 2 composites with tuned and broadband absorption towards low-frequency electromagnetic waves
abstract: Engineering microwave absorption composites with tuned and broadband absorption towards low-frequency electromagnetic waves of both civil and military areas.

title: Ion-doped lead-free double perovskite Cs 2 NaBiCl 6 with multiple excitation and tunable emission towards light emitting and anti-counterfeiting applications
abstract: Ion doped lead-free double perovskite Cs 2 NaBiCl 6 with multiple excitation and tunable emission was fabricated towards light emitting (CNBC:30%Br − , 15%Mn 2+ ) and anti-counterfeiting applications (CNBC:30%Br − , 15%Mn 2+ , 12%Er 3+ , 12%Yb 3+ ), respectively.

title: High-performance carbon nanofiber conductive films induced by titanium carbide
abstract: Our study revolutionizes electrothermal film fabrication, pioneering the synthesis of titanium carbide (TiC)/carbon nanofiber (CNF) composites via electrospinning, providing a nuanced understanding of chemical structure transformations.

title: Precise patterning of gold nanoparticle gratings on gelatin films
abstract: A method (based on holographic lithography, dry etching and temperature annealing) to fabricate gold nanoparticles in gelatin films and pattern them into gratings with periods down to 200 nm, controlling the size and particle distribution.

title: Investigation of local structure and phase recovery in an irradiated Nd 2 Zr 2 O 7 pyrochlore
abstract: SHI irradiation-induced order–disorder transition in an Nd 2 Zr 2 O 7 pyrochlore is observed by XRD and XAS measurements. The recrystallization of the irradiated phase was observed as a result of thermal annealing.

title: Understanding the growth mechanisms of γ-GeSe for polymorph-selective large-area deposition
abstract: This work unveils the pivotal roles of Au catalysts and substrates in the polymorph-selective, large-area synthesis of γ-GeSe. The demonstrated synthesis of γ-GeSe will advance energy storage and optoelectronics applications with the new polymorph.

title: Tailoring the properties of semi-aromatic copolyimides through structural manipulation towards energy-storage applications
abstract: Three series of copolyimides with various polar units such as nitrile, carbonyl, polyethylene and polyethylene oxide were developed and tested as flexible/stretchable dielectric materials or energy storage capacitors.

title: Excellent red upconversion luminescence in GdLaO 3 :Er 3+ /Yb 3+ /Sc 3+ under 980 nm laser excitation
abstract: Er 3+ /Yb 3+ are distributed in a double-layer structure, blocked by a single layer of Sc 3+ /La 3+ . Red UCL intensity of (Gd 0.8 Er 0.1 Yb 0.1 )(La 0.9 Sc 0.1 )O 3 is comparable to β-NaYF 4 :Er 3+ /Yb 3+ .

title: Ca 18 Na 3 Y(PO 4 ) 14 :Eu 2+ ,Mn 2+ : a novel single-phase dual-emission phosphor and its spectral modulation by Mg 2+ for plant lighting and WLEDs
abstract: The luminescent properties of Ca 18 Na 3 Y(PO 4 ) 14 :Eu 2+ ,Mn 2+ and its spectral modulation by Mg 2+ for plant lighting and WLEDs are discussed.

title: Silk fibroin/graphene quantum dots composite memristor with multi-level resistive switching for synaptic emulators
abstract: Silk fibroin (SF)/graphene quantum dots (GQDs) composite memristor realizes three resistance states in single scanning cycle and has the ability to simulate synaptic plasticity.

title: High-performance and environmentally friendly circularly polarized light direct detection based on ZnO nanowires and chiral cellulose nanocrystals
abstract: The CPL photodetector composed of a thin film self-assembled from CNC and ZnONWAs has different light responses under RCPL and LCPL irradiation. The detector also has a high asymmetry factor and can be applied to CPL detection.

title: Nanocrystalline cellulose-based mixed ionic–electronic conductor for bioelectronics
abstract: A mixed ionic–electronic conductor (MIEC) comprising sulfated cellulose nanocrystals (S-CNCs) as nanotemplate is investigated in organic electrochemical transistors (OECTs) with remarkable channel current and high transconductance values.

title: A passivation strategy assisting a robust and low consumption power BP-based optical synaptic device for neural computing
abstract: This study advances artificial synapses with BP–GOQDs, showcasing stability and bio-visual recognition, which are promising for commercializing BP-based neural devices and opening new applications.

title: Green-emitting CsPbI 3 nanorods decorated with CsPb 2 I 5 and Cs 4 PbI 6 nanoclusters
abstract: This work presents green-emitting CsPbI 3 nanorods decorated Cs 4 PbI 6 and CsPb 2 I 5 nanoclusters with a color-tuning approach via multiple-phase combinations without changing elements. These materials have stable emission and color purity.

title: Halide-dependent humidity sensing of Cs 2 SnX 6 (X = Cl, Br, I) perovskites for real-time human physiological moisture detection
abstract: Lead-free halide perovskites Cs 2 SnX 6 (X = Cl, Br, I) exhibit favorable humidity sensing with a response sequence of Cs 2 SnCl 6 > Cs 2 SnBr 6 > Cs 2 SnI 6 , enabling great potential for real-time non-contact human breath and finger monitoring.

title: A superhydrophobic MOF facilitating efficient solvent-free catalytic chemical fixation of CO 2 and oxidation of hydrocarbons and MOF@cotton@starch composite-based selective sensing of a herbicide
abstract: Superhydrophobic Zr-MOF unveils solvent-free chemical fixation of CO 2 at 1 bar, along with efficient aerobic oxidation of hydrocarbons into fine chemicals and fast, nanomolar detection of aclonifen in wastewater, vegetables, and fruits.

title: An attachable, self-healing and durable TiO 2 /rGO/PVA photocatalytic hydrogel band for dye degradation
abstract: A photocatalytic hydrogel band with the advantages of being bendable, attachable, self-healing, and durable, and high photocatalytic activity for dye degradation was fabricated by facile processes.

title: Boosting lateral photosensing performances in a P(VDF-TrFE)/Bi 2 Se 3 /Si heterojunction induced by surface modification and ferroelectric and pyroelectric effects
abstract: A broadband high-sensitivity self-powered PSD is developed by using a Bi 2 Se 3 /Si heterojunction and a P(VDF-TrFE) surface functional layer, and analyzed by reducing surface electrical conductivity and coupling ferroelectric and pyroelectric effects.

title: Water-dispersed bismuth-doped strontium pyrovanadate phosphor particles with sub-degree celsius thermal resolution
abstract: The lifetime and emission of water-dispersed Sr 2 V 2 O 7 :Bi 3+ exhibit strong thermal quenching around ambient temperatures. The high-temperature sensitivity and microsecond lifetime of this material make it suitable for temperature imaging applications.

title: An adhesive, low swelling and conductive tri-network hydrogel for wearable electronic devices
abstract: This study provides a strategy to generate stretchable, compressive, and conductive hydrogels with adhesive properties and can be used in underwater environments, expanding the potential applications of hydrogels as wearable electronic devices.

title: Large-area in situ growth of a flexible perovskite scintillator film for X-ray indirect detection applications
abstract: A large-area ultra-stable in situ grown vitamin-doped perovskite film and its application in X-ray photodetection.

title: Supramolecular covalent cellulose-based bioplastics with high transparency, hydrophobicity, ionic conductivity, mechanical robustness, and recyclability
abstract: A novel CBP with good optical transmittance, hydrophobicity, mechanical toughness, heat sealability, ionic conductivity, and recyclability is reported.

title: Controlled growth of 3D topological insulator BiSb(Te 1− y Se y ) 3 nanocrystals via chemical vapor transport
abstract: The structural and electrical properties of thin nanocrystals of the 3D topological insulator BiSb(Te 1− y Se y ) 3 ( y = 0, 0.01, 0.02, …, 0.09) have been investigated.

title: Superior energy storage properties in lead-free NaNbO 3 -based relaxor antiferroelectric ceramics via a combined optimization strategy
abstract: A stable relaxor AFE R phase in NaNbO 3 -based ceramics is achieved. An excellent W rec of ∼11.09 J cm −3 and an η of ∼85.7% were realized at 1200 kV cm −1 for x = 0.30 thick film ceramic.

title: An in situ study on the depth-resolved chemical states of undoped SrTiO 3 (001) surfaces during Ar + sputtering and annealing processes with XPS
abstract: During Ar + sputtering and UHV annealing processes on undoped SrTiO 3 (001) surfaces, the interactions between SrO and impurity ions can be witnessed from their Sr 3d spectra.

title: Ionic liquid-assisted growth of high-quality methylammonium lead bromide single crystals for photodetection applications
abstract: The addition of a 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide (BMIB) ionic liquid to the MAPbBr 3 precursor solution shows a significant influence on the crystal optoelectronic properties and photodetection performance.

title: Progress and challenges: a review of ionic liquid treatment for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: While perovskite solar cells (PSCs) have achieved remarkable power conversion efficiencies (PCEs) exceeding 26.1%, significant challenges remain in enhancing their efficiency and stability for commercial viability.

title: Recent progress in the fabrication strategies and toughening mechanism of flexible ceramics and their applications
abstract: This review article offers a comprehensive overview of toughening methods and mechanisms for flexible ceramic materials, highlighting potential applications and future opportunities.

title: Bio-inspired, robust, and anti-swelling hydrogel sensors for underwater information transmission
abstract: An anti-swelling supramolecular hydrogel with robustness, fast self-recovery, and strain-sensitivity was meticulously engineered through leveraging the principles of multiple dynamic interaction matching.

title: Sodium hypodiphosphate – a new ionic conductor. Synthesis, stability, conductivity and 3D electron diffraction crystal structure
abstract: The structure and ionic conductivity of a novel material, sodium hypodiphosphate, are reported along with a comparison with known analogues.

title: Modulating luminescence properties of CsMnBr 3 -based lead-free single crystals by pseudohalide doping
abstract: Pseudohalide doping promoting luminescence properties of lead-free CsMnBr 3 -based crystals.

title: Thermal degradation in methylammonium–formamidinium–guanidinium lead iodide perovskites
abstract: Thermal degradation experiments in GA x FA y MA 1− x − y PbI 3 perovskites show that mixed compositions are generally more stable, but not always. Large GA + cations may decrease the stability and even change the order of the degradation kinetics.

title: Eggshell protein modified cotton fabric for flexible and sustainable piezoelectric wearable sensors
abstract: A fabric-based piezoelectric sensor with eggshell protein is constructed as a potential candidate for green and high-performance electronic devices.

title: Eco-friendly lead-free halide double perovskites A 2 CuMCl 6 (A = K, Rb; M = Sb, Bi): stability, thermoelectric, and optoelectronic advancements through theoretical insights
abstract: Lead-free halide A 2 CuMCl 6 (A = K, Rb; M = Sb, Bi) are analyzed for stability, thermoelectric, and optoelectronic properties. Band gaps of 1.24–1.53 eV and high zT values (≈1.05–1.00) demonstrate their potential for renewable energy and optoelectronic applications.

title: Low-temperature sintering of Cu@Ag microparticles in air for recyclable printed electronics
abstract: An optimal temperature for printed conductors based on silver-coated copper microparticles is 160–250 °C, leading to silver sinter necks that enhance the conductivity. Treatments are done in air and the particles can be recycled from the prints.

title: Synthesis and properties of La 18 Fe 5 Cu 4 S 26 O 8 , a compound containing large magnetic clusters
abstract: La 18 Fe 5 Cu 4 S 26 O 8 , a novel quinary compound exhibiting a crystal structure with both ordered and disordered features is reported. The compound exhibits cluster-glass magnetic properties, as a consequence of its unusual structure.

title: Morphological evolution of individual microrods to self-assembled 3D hierarchical flower architectures of CuBi x In 1− x Se 2 for photo response applications
abstract: CuBi x In 1− x Se 2 3D hierarchical microrod-based flowers were prepared by microwave-mediated method. The morphology tuning for different Bi/In content shows optical and electrical properties variation with better white light photo response applications.

title: Alkyl modification to optimize the ferroelastic properties of molecular crystals
abstract: Using alkyl modification, we obtained a ferroelastic material with three phase transitions, orange fluorescence and high-temperature ferroelasticity.

title: Ferroelectric–ferromagnetic multiferroicity driven by 3d-electrons in a TiCrO 4 monolayer
abstract: Ferroelectric–ferromagnetic multiferroicity driven by 3d-electrons in TiCrO 4 monolayer documents the feasibility of the design strategy by doping magnetic elements in the ferroelectrics, as in the case of dilute magnetic semiconductors.

title: Effects of solution processable CuI thin films with Al 2 O 3 -based sandwiched architecture for high-performance p-type transistor applications
abstract: The enhancement in electrical performance of the solution-processed p-type CuI TFTs is achieved by constructing the aluminum oxide-based sandwiched architecture, which can improve the crystallinity and anion vacancy states in the CuI channel layer.

title: Correction: Preparation of phase-pure MgTiO 3 microwave dielectric ceramics for GPS antenna application
abstract: Correction for ‘Preparation of phase-pure MgTiO 3 microwave dielectric ceramics for GPS antenna application’ by Dhananjay Mali et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2024, 12 , 15101–15111, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TC02724H.

title: Dual-anchored configuration involving Pb(NO 3 ) 2 for effective and stable FAPbI 3 quantum dot solar cells
abstract: A novel surface passivation method using lead salts forms a stable O–C···O–N structure between NO−3 and FA⁺ ions, enhancing environmental stability and achieving 12.11% photoelectric conversion efficiency in FAPbI 3 quantum dot solar cells.

title: Efficient and stable perovskite solar cells based on insulating polymer modified ZnO nanoparticles
abstract: This approach, combining an insulating polymer with ZnO NPs, offers a simple and economical pathway towards the commercialization of PSCs.

title: Dynamic memristor array with multiple reservoir states for training efficient neuromorphic computing
abstract: Exploration of efficient neuromorphic computing using Pt/Al/TiO y /TiO x /Al 2 O 3 /Pt array memristors implemented a reservoir with 16 states, demonstrating the training process of synaptic array images.

title: High-efficiency organic solar cells enabled by nonfullerene acceptors with varying alkyloxy substitution positions of the phenyl outer side chains
abstract: Two isomeric molecules named m -BTP-OEH and p -BTP-OEH were developed by attaching a 2-ethylhexyloxyl substituent at the meta - or para -position of its phenyl side chains and a PCE promotion from 17.23% to 18.02% was achieved.

title: Stretchable and stable neuromorphic tactile system
abstract: A performance-stable tactile neuron is developed, which integrates a stretch-insensitive triboelectric nanogenerator with an artificial neuron in a single device, and a 64 × 64 neuromorphic tactile matrix is established to process touch signals.

title: Advances in the construction of photonic structures with dual stopbands and beyond
abstract: The advances in developing multiple stopband photonic structures are reviewed, which can help researchers to develop photonic structures with multiple stopbands and tackle the challenges to meet the demands for anticounterfeiting, etc .

title: Thermogalvanic organohydrogel-based non-contact self-powered electronics for advancing smart agriculture
abstract: Non-contact self-powered electronics are proposed based on a thermoelectric hydrogel, which can be applied in remote-controlled small electronics, wireless information interaction and monitoring solar intensity via photo-thermal-electric conversion.

title: Multiple-resonant nitrogen embedded nanographenes with high photoluminescence efficiency and high colour purity
abstract: Two indolo[3,2,1- jk ]carbazole-based nanographenes (ICz-NGs) were designed and synthesized. Organic light-emitting diodes employing these ICz-NGs as emitters exhibited blue and green emissions with small full width at half maxima of 18–23 nm.

title: Structural evolution of a high-temperature piezoelectric Pb(In 1/2 Nb 1/2 )O 3 –PbTiO 3 ceramic under an in situ electric field
abstract: The PIN–0.36PT relaxor ferroelectric at the MPB exhibits reversible phase transformation and relatively flexible M structure, which enable large lattice strain and domain switching, and are responsible for the enhanced piezoelectric properties.

title: Highly efficient organic–graphene hybrid photodetectors via molecular peripheral editing
abstract: A molecular peripheral editing approach based on tetrathiafulvalene molecules is presented to elucidate the structure–property relationship of organic molecules, toward high-performance photodetectors based on organic–graphene hybrids.

title: Facile in situ synthesis of double perovskite Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 /WS 2 heterostructure and interfacial charge transfer mediated high-performance ultraviolet photodetection
abstract: Pb-free halide perovskites have garnered enormous attention in optoelectronics. We report a facile in-situ synthesis of double perovskite Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 /WS 2 heterostructure and study its high-performance ultraviolet photodetection in a planar geometry.

title: Negative permittivity enhanced reflection and adsorption of electromagnetic waves from carbon fiber felt/carbon nanotubes
abstract: Negative permittivity generated by plasma oscillations enhances the electromagnetic interference reflection and absorption performance of CFF/CNTs through strong impedance mismatch and multiple loss mechanisms.

title: Enhanced capacitive energy storage of NaNbO 3 -based relaxor via modulating the phase structure strategy for high power energy storage
abstract: A schematic diagram of the design strategy to achieve high energy storage performance via the phase structure strategy: (a) and (b) dielectric temperature spectra, (c) PFM phase images, and (d) slim unipolar P–E hysteresis loop.

title: On-surface growth of transition-metal cobalt nanoclusters using a 2D crown-ether array
abstract: We report an on-surface growth of transition-metal nanoclusters using a 2D self-assembled monolayer (SAM) crown ether ring molecular array prepared on Cu(111).

title: The synthesis of MOF nanosheets and their application in MOF-based resistance random access memory devices
abstract: This review introduces the synthesis methods of MOF nanosheets, discusses the application of MOF materials in RRAM memory devices, and explores the challenges and prospects of MOFs in developing RRAM memory devices.

title: A self-healing elastomer with outstanding mechanical properties was designed based on urea bonds
abstract: A high-strength polyurethane elastomer was synthesized by introducing functional segments containing thiourea and oxime–urethane bonds. The colorless transparent material may be used as electronic skin and flexibly self-healing sensors.

title: Electron transfer and energy exchange between a covalent organic framework and CuFeS 2 nanoparticles
abstract: The graphic shows the structural representation of the electrostatic assembly of oppositely charged CuFeS 2 nanoparticles with COF layers. Their addition in the COFs is enhancing the absorption capacity, while quenching the photoluminescence.

title: Enhanced band-tuning and quality of Ni-doped Ga 2 O 3 films via low-power RF magnetron sputtering
abstract: The proposed mechanism for the conductivity and band tuning in Ni-doped Ga 2 O 3 films depends on the presence of oxygen vacancies and Ni ions. The Ni-doped Ga 2 O 3 films were prepared using an extremely low power density of 0.18–0.36 W cm −2 .

title: Pyroelectricity in poled all-organic polar polynorbornene/polydimethylsiloxane-based stretchable electrets
abstract: A novel all-organic polar elastic composite consisting of a polar polynorbornene-based filler in a PDMS matrix shows room temperature non-linear pyroelectricity due to Maxwell–Wagner interfacial polarization and secondary pyroelectricity.

title: Sb-enhanced Cs 3 Cu 2 I 5 scintillators for ionizing radiation detection
abstract: Lead-free perovskite composites were prepared by in situ doping and crystallization. The optimized scintillators achieved improved stability, light yield and transparency, which resulted high X-ray sensitivity and low detection limit.

title: Manipulating molecular orientation in vapor-deposited organic semiconductor glasses via in situ electric fields: a molecular dynamics study
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulations of vapor-deposited glasses under the effect of an external electric field show that it is possible to detangle molecular orientation and density/stability of the glass.

title: From fundamentals to frontiers: a review of memristor mechanisms, modeling and emerging applications
abstract: The escalating demand for artificial intelligence (AI), the internet of things (IoTs), and energy-efficient high-volume data processing has brought the need for innovative solutions to the forefront.

title: On-chip two-dimensional material-based waveguide-integrated photodetectors
abstract: In optical systems for communication, sensing, and imaging, integrating optoelectronic and electronic components on-chip to develop optoelectronic applications has become the focus of future research.

title: Ambient solid-state triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion in ureasil organic–inorganic hybrid hosts
abstract: Solid-state green-to-blue upconversion is shown in organic–inorganic hybrid ureasil hosts using the palladium( ii ) octaethylporphyrin and diphenylanthracene sensitizer/emitter pair, with emission retained for >70 days without deoxygenation.

title: Unexpected p-type thermoelectric transport arising from magnetic Mn substitution in Fe 2 V 1− x Mn x Al Heusler compounds
abstract: An anomalous p-type thermopower was realised for nominally n-doped Fe 2 V 1− x Mn x Al, via localized magnetic Mn impurity states at the band edges. A relatively large power factor 2.2 mW K −2 m −1 and ZT ∼ 0.1 could be obtained for the p-type full Heuslers.

title: Efficient transparent blue-emitted perovskite light-emitting diodes based on multilayer transparent top electrodes
abstract: A blue emitted transparent perovskite light-emitting diode was prepared by using quasi two-dimensional perovskite materials and multilayer transparent top electrodes.

title: Extending the absorption of Cs 2 AgBiCl 6 double perovskite to the near infra-red region by copper doping
abstract: In this study, we establish the synthesis of Cu doped Cs 2 AgBiCl 6 lead-free double perovskite using three different methods. Cu doping results in extending the absorption window of high bandgap Cs 2 AgBiCl 6 double perovskite to NIR region.

title: PVA/KGM dual network hydrogels doped with carbon nanotube-collagen corona as flexible sensors for human motion monitoring
abstract: PVA/KGM dual-network hydrogel doped with carbon nanotube-collagen corona has excellent temperature adaptability as a flexible strain sensor for stable monitoring of human motion.

title: Multifaceted classification and integration of time-varying complex signals using analog neuromorphic UV phototransistors
abstract: We present neuromorphic optical sensor for multifaceted classification. It discriminates 310, 365, and 395 nm wavelengths, tracks illumination duration and termination, real-time dynamic sensing, and monitors complex phenomena like water vortices.

title: A universal supramolecular assembly strategy for achieving efficient tunable white emission and anti-counterfeiting in antimony doped tin( iv )-based vacancy-ordered double perovskites
abstract: Three compounds with high PLQY, (AC) 2 SnCl 6 :Sb 3+ (A = K, Rb, and Cs), were developed using a supramolecular assembly strategy. The samples doped with Sb 3+ were used to achieve tunable WLEDs, anti-counterfeiting, and information encryption.

title: Tuning the performance of PSCs using rare-earth elements
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) are emerging and promising alternatives to the market-leading solar cells due to their high performance, low fabrication cost, and versatile material modification.

title: Chiral self-assembled (β, d )-RIB-TPE micron vesicles with AIE characteristics used as targeting-drug carriers
abstract: The synthetic material (β, d )-RIB-TPE, which can realize the specific recognition of the drug S -(+)-clopidogrel sulfate. The drug S -(+)-clopidogrel sulfate could be embedded, and the embedding rate was 45.7%.

title: A muconic acid derivative isomeric organic–inorganic metal halide with high-temperature phase transition
abstract: ( Z , Z )- and ( E , E )-[BPHD]ZnCl 4 are the first muconic acid derivative organic–inorganic metal halides, in which the EE -isomer exhibits a high phase transition temperature of up to 449.4 K.

title: Silicon-based peripheral steric donor modifications for a high-efficiency multi-resonance thermally activated delayed fluorescence emitter
abstract: Multi-resonance thermally activated delayed fluorescence (MR-TADF) emitters have broad applications in organic light-emitting diodes (OLEDs).

title: Effect of Sn valence state in the in situ growth of FeOOH precursor on the performance of the α-Fe 2 O 3 photoanode
abstract: Sn-doped Fe 2 O 3 photoanodes are fabricated by employing a tin salt precursor with different Sn valence states, and Sn 4+ ions are incorporated in the Fe 2 O 3 photoanode.

title: A review on diverse streams of interface engineering for organic thin-film transistors
abstract: This review article provides a critical overview of the diverse technological streams of interface engineering formed in the field of OTFTs.

title: Adamantyl ligand-induced one-dimensional CsPbBr 3 perovskite nanocrystal formation: mechanism and anisotropic photoluminescence
abstract: Perovskite nanocrystalline materials have good development prospects in the fields of solar cells, photoelectric detection, fluorescent sensors and light-emitting diodes.

title: Resolving charge transfer mechanisms in molecular tunnel junctions using dynamic charge transfer and static current–voltage measurements
abstract: Comparing the tunnelling decay coefficient β values obtained through the core-hole clock and junction J ( V ) measurements allow for the identification of the most likely orbital involved in charge transport measurements, derived from DFT calculations.

title: High-κ elastomer with dispersed ferroelectric nematic liquid crystal microdroplets
abstract: An emergent liquid ferroelectric material is dispersed into polydimethylsiloxane as microdroplets, realizing a stretchable and ferroelectric high-κ elastomer.

title: The role of spontaneous orientation polarization on charge storage behavior at an interface between organic semiconductor layers
abstract: Optically generated carriers are pinned at the organic/organic interface if the two organic layers have a large polarization difference, underlining that the combination of polar molecules is crucial to extend the carrier lifetime.

title: Unlocking color-tunable emission of Eu 2+ -activated phosphors through doping-free exploration of hidden sites
abstract: Reducing atmosphere modification (a doping-free strategy) leads to Eu 2+ emission color changes from blue to cyan.

title: Band alignment of TiO 2 by controling Cl content for high-efficiency perovskite solar cells
