abstract: This study presents, for the first time, a fully printable mesoporous indium tin oxide (ITO) perovskite light-emitting diode (ITO-PeLED).

title: Electrochemical host–guest interactions in a disordered oligosilyl coordination polymer
abstract: A disordered zirconium-oligosilyl coordination polymer exhibits coordination-dependent charge transfer.

title: Construction of a SnS 2 /TiO 2 S-scheme heterostructure photocatalyst for highly efficient photocatalytic degradation of tetracycline hydrochloride
abstract: A schematic diagram of the catalytic mechanism of photocatalytic degradation of tetracycline hydrochloride pollutant by Sn 2 /TiO 2 S-scheme heterostructure photocatalysts in water.

title: Orbital-engineered anomalous Hall conductivity in stable full Heusler compounds: a pathway to optimized spintronics
abstract: Through high-throughput DFT, we explore AHC in 2904 full Heusler compounds, identifying several with superior AHC values, emphasizing the impact of valence electrons and electronegativity, and revealing their potential for spintronic applications.

title: The effect of hydrogen bond interactions on the electronic nature of DPP-based organic semiconductors: implications on charge transport
abstract: Tailoring hydrogen bonds as a mean to modulate the electronic characteristics of small DPP semiconductors, facilitating charge formation and enhancing charge transport properties in devices.

title: Environmentally stable Mn-doped CsPbX 3 @CsPb 2 X 5 core–shell materials with efficient energy transfer
abstract: CsPbX 3 @CsPb 2 X 5 composites were obtained in one step by controlling the reaction time and precursor ratio, and their energy transfer efficiency and stability were significantly improved benefiting from the protection of the CsPb 2 X 5 shell.

title: Thieno[3,2- b ]phosphole-based AIEgens: facile preparation and dual modulation of solid-state luminescence
abstract: This study highlights the adaptability of thieno[3,2-b]phosphole-based fluorophores for tailored solid-state luminescence. Strategic manipulation of molecular electronic structure and intermolecular interactions resulted in a variety of innovative materials.

title: 2D/1D PbI 2 /Sb 2 S 3 van der Waals heterojunction for highly sensitive and broadband photodetectors
abstract: A 2D/1D PbI 2 /Sb 2 S 3 van der Waals heterojunction was successfully used to construct a photodetector device with high sensitivity and broad spectral response.

title: Facile synthesis of benzothiadiazole and its derivative-based covalent organic frameworks using “two-in-one” monomers for photocatalytic hydrogen generation
abstract: Four benzothiadiazole and its derivative-based imine-type COFs have been successfully constructed using “two-in-one” monomers for efficient photocatalytic hydrogen generation.

title: Photoluminescence study of perovskite materials derived from tungsten- and lanthanide-containing aurivillius layered perovskite, Bi 2 Na 0.63 Ln 0.37 Ta 1.75 W 0.25 O 9
abstract: The structure and photoluminescence properties of a W-containing perovskite nanosheet derived from an Aurivillius phase layered perovskite Bi 2 Na 0.63 Ln 0.37 Ta 1.75 W 0.25 O 9 (Ln = La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Eu, Gd, Tb, Sm, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, and Yb) were investigated.

title: Layered methylhydrazinium lead halide perovskites: new crystal polymorphs with a tailored band gap and photoluminescence colour via halide substitution
abstract: The Ruddlesden–Popper lead halide HOIPs exhibit rich temperature-driven polymorphism, bandgap adjustability, and shift of photoluminescence colour via the halide-mixing approach. This is the first report of a 3D perovskite phase with MHy + .

title: Benzobisthiazole unit in 4,8-connection mode to build D–A polymer donors achieving high short-circuit current density for organic solar cells
abstract: In this work, a series of D–A conjugated polymer donors (PBDT-BBTH, PBDT-BBTF and PBDT-BBTCl) was designed based on the benzobisthiazole (BBT) unit in the 4,8-connection mode with the benzodithiophene (BDT) unit linked by the thiophene π-bridge.

title: Memristive effects within stacking faults consisting of locally coexisting rhombohedral and Bernal lattices in exfoliated graphite and multilayered carbon nano-onion
abstract: We report an in-depth investigation of stacking faults in exfoliated pyrolytic graphite and in multilayered lattices of carbon nano-onions by employing a combination of atomic force microscopy (AFM) and Raman point and mapping spectroscopy.

title: Zinc complex-based hole transporting material for perovskite solar cell applications
abstract: Developing hole-transport materials (HTMs) with high hole mobility is critical for constructing efficient perovskite solar cells (PSCs).

title: Alloying strategy for developing a single-band warm white emitting material Cs 2 NaGdCl 6 :Bi 3+ via Ag + co-doping
abstract: A single-band warm white light covering almost the entire visible spectrum was obtained via alloying with Ag + in a rare-earth-based inorganic perovskite crystal CNAGC:Bi 3+ .

title: The heterostructure topology of a chemiresistive sensor based on hexagonal BaTiO 3 and 2D SnO for toluene detection
abstract: The SnO–BaTiO 3 bi-layered heterostructured chemiresistive sensor exhibits promising potential for detecting volatile organic compounds (VOCs) under ambient conditions.

title: Boosting green emission in Mn( ii )-doped Cs 3 ZnCl 5 by introducing an energy transfer mediator
abstract: An alcohothermal method is introduced for synthesizing doped Cs 3 ZnCl 5 phosphors. The photoluminescence quantum yield of Cs 3 ZnCl 5 :Mn 2+ was enhanced from 51.6% to 71.1% by codoping with Cu + , which acts as an effective energy transfer mediator.

title: Heteroepitaxial tuning of resonant forbidden reflections in a spinel
abstract: Resonant elastic X-ray scattering at the V K edge reveals systematic variations of the resonant forbidden (002) reflection in thin films of spinel LiV 2 O 4 deposited on MgAl 2 O 4 , SrTiO 3 , and MgO.

title: Correction: Effect of the cyano group on colour-tunability of aryl-substituted buta-1,3-diene based solid-state emissive copolymers
abstract: Correction for ‘Effect of the cyano group on colour-tunability of aryl-substituted buta-1,3-diene based solid-state emissive copolymers’ by Prasanta Pal et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2023, 11 , 16594–16604, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TC02999A.

title: Dynamic monitoring of the light-soaking effect of organic–inorganic perovskite solar cells doped with alkali metal ions
abstract: This work investigates the light-soaking effects in perovskites using time-dependent photoluminescence spectroscopy. Alkali metal ion doping can enhance the photobrightning effect in perovskites by slowing down ion migration.

title: Tri-layered Si/Co 3 O 4 /ZnO heterojunction for high-performance visible photodetection
abstract: Tri-layered heterojunction devices based on oxide thin films are attracting significant attention for ultra-fast visible photodetection.

title: Nanoscale stability behavior of the CH 3 NH 3 PbI 3 perovskite via scanning thermal chemical microscopy
abstract: Scanning thermal chemical microscopy offers an approach for investigating nanoscale stability behavior of CH 3 NH 3 PbI 3 by recording nanoscale temperature-dependent IR spectra and thermal chemical mapping.

title: High-efficiency narrowband multi-resonance TADF emitters via the introduction of bulky adamantane units
abstract: New design strategy for MR-TADF emitters by incorporating bulky adamantane unit as steric hindrance into MR skeletons to mitigate intermolecular π–π stacking and green organic light-emitting diodes with high EQE up to 32.3% and low roll-off are achieved.

title: The latest research progress of conductive hydrogels in the field of electrophysiological signal acquisition
abstract: Conductive hydrogels have great potential in the field of electrophysiological signal acquisition because of their outstanding properties.

title: Synchronous achievement of ultra-wideband microwave absorption and high thermal conduction in spongy TiO 2 -based magnetic composites via constructing magnetic/dielectric double loss and phonon/electron co-transmission
abstract: Spongy TiO 2 -based magnetic composites were synthesized as high-performance fillers with excellent EMW absorption and high thermal conduction via a hydrothermal-soaking-annealing route for the first time.

title: Electrochemical phase transition of ionic hydrogels
abstract: A general electrochemistry protocol is proposed which changes the local pH by electrode reactions and enables a spatially patterned phase transition on the surface of hydrogels, showing great potential in informatics, cryptology, and security areas.

title: Flexible and multifunctional composite films based on rare earth phosphors as broadband thermal emitters for high-performance passive radiative cooling
abstract: Flexible composite films based on rare earth phosphors can be used as broadband thermal emitters for passive radiative cooling and energy conservation.

title: Multilayer films for photon upconversion-driven photoswitching
abstract: The use of triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion (TTA-UC) to drive photoswitching of the energy-storing norbornadiene–quadricyclane (NBD–QC) isomers in the solid-state is demonstrated.

title: Improving the performance of lead-free Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 double perovskite solar cells by passivating Br vacancies
abstract: Targeted passivation of the easily formed deep level defect V Br by introducing 4% HBr results in an enhancement of PCE. This work enriches the study on the passivation of intrinsic point defects in Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 .

title: Influence of sintering characteristics and structural properties on the microwave dielectric properties of non-stoichiometric Ca 3 Mn 2+ x Ge 3 O 12+ δ ( x = 0–0.2) ceramics
abstract: The structural characteristics of Ca 3 Mn 2+ x Ge 3 O 12+ δ ( x = 0.1) ceramics correspond to their excellent microwave dielectric properties: ε r = 8.88, Q × f = 50 793 GHz, τ f = −39.35 ppm °C −1 .

title: Tunable near-infrared phosphor LiSc 2 SbO 6 :Cr 3+ by Mg 2+ substitution and Yb 3+ co-doping for phosphor-converted light-emitting diodes
abstract: The NIR pc-LED prepared with LMSS:Cr 3+ ,Yb 3+ performed well in night vision, nondestructive testing, multiple anti-counterfeiting and information encryption technologies.

title: Achieving time-dependent and color-tunable ultralong room temperature phosphorescence through sodiation reconfiguration for dynamic 5D information encryption
abstract: A metal–organic thermal polymerization strategy is developed for synthesizing ultralong-lived Al/Phe-PMs with time-dependent phosphorescence color (TDPC) and NaOH-stimulated color tunability, with a production capacity of kilogram scale.

title: Tailoring the optical properties of rubrene films through epitaxy-induced amorphous-to-crystal transition
abstract: We show that the amorphous-to-crystal transition driven by epitaxy is the process behind the growth of rubrene films with single-crystal-like properties. Growth optimisation expedites this transition and greatly improves the homogeneity of the films.

title: Rationally designed synthesis of bright Cu–Ga–Zn–Se-based nanocrystals for efficient green quantum-dot light-emitting diodes
abstract: Cd-free QLEDs with tunable emission from 603 to 524 nm were constructed by regulating the non-stoichiometric Cu : Ga molar ratio, and the green QLEDs showed high performance with an EQE of 5.8% and a high brightness of 7016 cd m −2 .

title: Unique switching mode of HfO 2 among fluorite-type ferroelectric candidates
abstract: The primary uniqueness of HfO 2 is its indispensible covalent bonding nature, though its high cation coordination number reflects a typical ionic compound.

title: Z -Scheme g-C 3 N 4 /TiO 2 heterojunction for a high energy density photo-assisted Li–O 2 battery
abstract: A lithium–oxygen battery based on the formation of lithium oxide (Li 2 O) can theoretically achieve a high energy density through a four-electron reaction.

title: Dual-port ferroelectric NAND flash memory for large memory window, QLC programmable and disturbance-free operations
abstract: Oxide channel dual-port FeNAND devices with low-temperature processing offer improved thermal stability, broader memory window, and enhanced reliability.

title: Carbon–Ni 6 Co 3 Fe 1 alloy hybrid foil for electromagnetic wave interference shielding in X-band and extremely low frequencies
abstract: A magnetic alloy film/graphite composite is developed for shielding electromagnetic waves in the X-band and extremely low frequency regimes, using a scalable electrodeposition process. Enhanced thermal and mechanical properties are also achieved.

title: Temperature dependent Raman study of antiferromagnetic CrPS 4
abstract: The temperature dependence of the polarized Raman spectrum of antiferromagnetic CrPS 4 suggests a correlation between the phonon modes and the magnetic phase transition.

title: Enhanced nonlinear optical properties of Au 25 nanocluster oligomers linked by bidentate dithiol
abstract: Oligomerization of atomically-precise clusters leads to enhancements of their nonlinear optical performance (9000 GM for monomers, 66 000 GM for trimers).

title: Pressure enables high-standard white light emission and significant emission enhancement in a 2D halide perovskite
abstract: A 2D OIHP, (CHA) 2 PbBr 4 , exhibits significant pressure-induced emission enhancement at 2.0 GPa. The luminescence color of the crystal changes from yellow to white under pressure.

title: Thienoimidazole-pyridine based small molecule hole transport materials for dopant-free, efficient inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: A new hole-transporting material based on heterocyclic thienoimidazole-pyridine is synthesized for perovskite solar cells with an 18.77% efficiency.

title: Chiral quantum dots for bioapplications
abstract: This article provides a comprehensive review of chiral quantum dots used for bioapplications. The chiral mechanisms, g factor and some bioapplications are also discussed.

title: Spin-gapless semiconducting characteristics and related band topology of quaternary Heusler alloy CoFeMnSn
abstract: This study presents the synthesis, structural analysis, magnetic behavior, transport properties, and electronic characteristics of a novel spin-gapless semiconductor compound CoFeMnSn within the quaternary Heusler alloy.

title: Realizing high zero-bias gain in a GaN-based bipolar phototransistor through thin-base configuration for ultraviolet imaging
abstract: A GaN-based ultraviolet bipolar phototransistor with high zero-bias gain is obtained based on an asymmetric n–p–i–n homo-structure. A thin-base structure is proposed to suppress excess holes recombination and, thus, to promote optical gain.

title: Thermal enhancement of the upconversion luminescence of Zn 2+ ion-doped KYb(MoO 4 ) 2 :Er 3+ phosphors for multimode temperature sensing
abstract: KYb(MoO 4 ) 2 :Er 3+ ,Zn 2+ exhibit multi-mode emission and thermal enhancement (TE). The proposed the TE mechanism and optical thermometry (OT) method open up a new way for UCL intensity TE theory and OT.

title: Chiral cadmium–amine complexes for stimulating non-linear optical activity and photoluminescence in solids based on aurophilic stacks
abstract: Cd( ii ) complexes with chiral amines play a dual role in materials incorporating aurophilic stacks, first, in breaking the symmetry to generate NLO activity, and second, in governing the Au⋯Au distances to give tunable charge transfer luminescence.

title: Ligand engineering of circularly polarized luminescence inversion and enhancement for chiral Ag 6 nanoclusters
abstract: Four pairs of chiral Ag 6 L 6 nanoclusters, synthesized using ligand engineering with thiazithione and oxazinthione, exhibit enhanced luminescence quantum yield attributed to S⋯S interactions and demonstrate supramolecular chirality inversion regulated by peripheral methyl and phenyl groups.

title: High synaptic plasticity enabled by controlled ion migration in organic heterojunction memristors
abstract: Fabrication of organic heterojunction memristor with controlled ion migration enables precise synaptic behavior simulation.

title: Spontaneous and reversible spin-splitting in ferroelectric A-type antiferromagnetism
abstract: We propose a possible way to produce spin-splitting in A-type antiferromagnetism with ferroelectric (FE) polarization in the absence of spin–orbital coupling (SOC). Moreover, the spontaneous spin-splitting in FE A-type antiferromagnetism is ferroelectrically controllable and reversible.

title: Single crystal synthesis and surface electronic structure of Bi 1.993 Cr 0.007 Se 3
abstract: Phase separation from a melt was used as a strategy to obtain diluted Cr-doped Bi 2 Se 3 bulk crystals. Large (∼500 μm × 500 μm) and chemically homogeneous Bi 1.993 Cr 0.007 Se 3 crystals were obtained displaying an energy gap at the Dirac point.

title: Colloidal quantum dots as single photon sources
abstract: Colloidal quantum dots (CQDs) are promising candidates for single photon sources (SPSs), pivotal for quantum technology. This review explores their applications, advancements, and potential in quantum photonics.

title: Halogen substitution of perinone-based cathode interfacial materials for high-efficiency inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: A fluorine-substituted perinone derivative exhibits better cathode interfacial modification than the chlorine-substituted one in inverted perovskite solar cells.

title: Tuning the electronic and optical properties of hg-C 3 N 4 quantum dots with edge-functionalization: a computational perspective
abstract: This study demonstrates the tuning of the vibrational, electronic, and optical properties of hg-C 3 N 4 quantum dots with edge-functionalization using density functional theory with the aim of exploring their possible applications in optoelectronics.

title: Construction of heterostructured SnS 2 /SnS@graphene oxide composite with highly effective microwave absorption
abstract: The RL min of SnS 2 /SnS@GO is −47.83 dB, and the EAB is 7.18 GHz at 2.00 mm.

title: X-ray-induced long persistent luminescence of Cu + -doped borosilicate glasses for radiation detection
abstract: This is the first report on the preparation of LPL glass through X-ray induction, and a highly linear relationship was observed between the initial afterglow intensity and the irradiation dose. It can be used for radiation dose detection.

title: Tailoring quantum transport efficiency in molecular junctions via doping of graphene electrodes
abstract: The efficiency of charge transport across a well-ordered molecular array is influenced not only by the molecular structure but also by the state of the electrode.

title: Phosphine oxide based semiconducting small molecule as an additive and an electron transport layer enables efficient and stable perovskite light-emitting devices
abstract: Inclusion of phosphine oxide-based PO-T2T molecule as an additive during perovskite crystallization yields finer perovskite grains and the same molecule acts as an electron transport layer enabling efficient and stable perovskite light emitting diodes.

title: Surface pre-sacrifice behavior of thiourea-based 2D perovskite s for high-performance perovskite solar cells
abstract: A surface pre-sacrifice strategy was developed to passivate interface defects and stabilize 3D perovskite by introducing 1,2-dimethylisothiourea cation. The prepared high-quality 2D/3D perovskite device shows an enhanced PCE of 22.26%.

title: Carbon-enveloped pea-shaped vanadium nitride nanorods for aqueous zinc ion batteries
abstract: AZIBs with VNC as the cathode electrode: as the reaction proceeds, H + and Zn 2+ undergo directional movement.

title: Stimuli-responsive circularly polarized luminescence with chiroptical amplification and inversion enabled by cholesterically assembled bio-materials
abstract: CPL active materials with a tunable g lum -factor, from −0.30 to 0.04, were fabricated by incorporating responsive organic dye into cellulose nanocrystals. Such films showed dynamic switchable CPL signal in intensity and handedness at different pH.

title: Flexible metal oxide thin-film transistors produced by a nanofiber-to-film process
abstract: The high-performance flexible thin-film transistors (TFTs) based on In 2 O 3 /Al 2 O 3 were fabricated by a nanofiber-to-film (NTF) process, and the excellent mechanical stability and reproducibility of the device were demonstrated.

title: A selectively bimodal flexible sensor based on IL/SWCNTs/PEDOT:PSS nanocomposites for materials and shape recognition
abstract: Inspired by the function of human skin, a flexible tactile sensor was assembled based on the novel IL/SWCNT/PEDOT:PSS nanocomposite, which possesses the multisensory ability to independently identify pressure and temperature with no cross-coupling.

title: Development of thermally stable FTO thin films on quartz substrates for carrier collection in semiconductor photoelectrodes
abstract: FTO/quartz prepared by spray pyrolysis maintained both low resistivity and high transmittance at temperatures up to 700 °C, enabling superior photoelectrochemical activity of CuBi 2 O 4 semiconductor photoelectrodes.

title: Bis(corannulenyl)ethene as an efficient photochromic material
abstract: A newly synthesized photochromic bis(corannulenyl)ethene shows high quantum yields for photocyclization and cycloreversion, with a sum of over 1, due to curved skeletons with several atropisomers. Photochromism in a polymer film is also demonstrated.

title: Reactive spin coating based on real-time in situ feedback for improved control of perovskite thin film fabrication
abstract: This work presents a novel perovskite thin film fabrication approach based on an optical in situ spectroscopy setup and a live closed-loop feedback system to improve the reproducibility of the layer properties independent of external parameters.

title: Exploring π-extended chiral ligands for the direct detection of circularly polarized light in 2D chiral perovskites
abstract: In this work, we synthesized π-extended phenyl based chiral organic ligands and successfully constructed larger-sized chiral perovskites, enabling the detection of circularly polarized light.

title: Plasma Ag nanoparticles loaded on Bi 2 MoO 6 to enhance surface oxygen vacancies for efficient nitrogen conversion to ammonia
abstract: Ag metal-loaded Bi 2 MoO 6 exhibited much higher photocatalytic nitrogen fixation activity than that of pure Bi 2 MoO 6 due to the surface plasmon resonance effect of its Ag NPs and enhanced oxygen vacancy concentration.

title: Superconductivity and superionic behaviors of Kagome lattices in Li–Si compounds under high pressure
abstract: P – T phase diagrams of the superconducting, solid, superionic, and liquid states in the Li 5 Si- P 6/ mmm at 50 and 100 GPa.

title: Quaternary CsPbX 3 (X = Cl 1− x Br x , Br 1− x I x ) alloy microplates synthesized by single-step chemical vapor deposition and their two-photon absorption (TPA) properties
abstract: Because of their well-defined light–matter interaction volume, high-quality single-crystalline nature, and precise bandgap tunability, all-inorganic cesium lead halide (CsPbX 3 (X = Cl, Br, I)) perovskite (IHP) microplates are of fundamental and technological interest today.

title: Dual functionality of NbO x memristors for synaptic and neuronal emulations in advanced neuromorphic systems
abstract: In this work, a novel NbO x memristor that significantly advances neuromorphic computing has been investigated by modulating the compliance current (CC). This device emulates the dynamic functionalities of artificial synapses and neurons.

title: Structural changes in HfSe 2 and ZrSe 2 thin films with various oxidation methods
abstract: Plasma oxidation is the most efficient method to form defect-free oxide layers on top of HfSe 2 and ZrSe 2 transition metal dichalcogenides, whereas thermal oxidation causes the formation of defects and cavities in the oxide.

title: Sulfhydryl functionalization of MXene enables selective sensing of mercury ions
abstract: Schematic representation of sulphydryl functionalization of MXene and selective sensing of mercury ions.

title: In situ laser-assisted decoration of Au nanoparticles on 3D porous graphene for enhanced 2-CEES sensing
abstract: In situ Au decorated laser-induced graphene (LIG/Au) behaves as an effective near-room-temperature 2-chloroethyl ethyl sulfide (2-CEES) sensor. It exhibits an enhanced response of 7.85‰ to 1.0 ppm 2-CEES, which is twice as that of pure LIG.

title: Defects and self-trapped exciton regulation in rare-earth doped all-inorganic perovskites
abstract: By controlling the growth temperature and increasing Er-doping concentration in CsPbCl 3 x Br 3(1− x ) :Er, their emitted PL can be tuned from red (1.82 eV) to near-infrared (1.53 eV) while keeping their intrinsic bandgap without significant variation.

title: Rational design of dimensionally matched 2D/2D COF based photocatalysts for highly efficient noble-metal-free solar energy catalysis
abstract: Ultrathin non-precious metal co-catalysts (WS2) were combined with a 2D COF (TP1C) through a solvothermal method, achieving excellent photocatalytic hydrogen production performance.

title: Oxygen heterocyclic coumarin-based hybridized local and charge-transfer deep-blue emitters for solution-processed organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Coumarin was employed as a new acceptor to construct the HLCT emitters. The solution-processed OLEDs exhibited deep-blue emission with the CIE coordinates of (0.16, 0.09), and the devices based on BCz-6P realized the champion EQE max of 6.80%.

title: Regulating the miscibility of donors/acceptors to manipulate the morphology and reduce non-radiative recombination energy loss enables efficient organic solar cells
abstract: The miscibility and crystallinity of fused ring electron acceptors is regulated to study the effects on the morphology and energy loss of organic solar cells (OSCs). BTP-C8 based OSCs juggle multiple impacts to gain the best efficiency.

title: Femtosecond laser annealing of fluorine-doped tin oxide films towards high-performance perovskite photovoltaics
abstract: Femtosecond laser annealing preparation of a superhydrophilic FTO surface helps promote the photoelectric conversion efficiency of perovskite solar cells to 22.33%.

title: Perovskite quantum dots embedded paper photodetectors with high flexibility and self-powered operation
abstract: Hybrid MAPbBr 3 quantum dot cellulose papers are fabricated via a one-step, oleic acid/oleylamine-free vacuum filtration method, and the corresponding photodetectors demonstrate self-powered capability, high flexibility, and exceptional stability.

title: Unveiling the influence of the boron clathrate lattice on superconductivity in a ternary Mg–La–B system
abstract: The boron clathrate lattice facilitates the superconductivity within the Mg–La–B system.

title: Laser processing of Li-doped mesoporous TiO 2 for ambient-processed mesoscopic perovskite solar cells
abstract: A rapid and scalable laser-assisted process to manufacture Li-doped mesoporous TiO 2 films for ambient-processed perovskite solar cells.

title: Harnessing lanthanides for blue-to-UVB upconversion and its dye-sensitization
abstract: An upconverter upconverts blue light into UVB photons, which can be used in dermatology to cure certain skin diseases. This concept mitigates the effects of UVB toxicity on healthy skin regions that may occur when direct UVB is administered.

title: Vacuum-assisted colossal enhancement of up-conversion luminescence of lanthanide-doped nanoparticles upon NIR laser irradiation – a new strategy for phosphor development
abstract: Permanent enhancement (by 2 orders of magnitude) of up-conversion luminescence via localized crystal growth, induced by NIR laser irradiation under vacuum conditions. This effect is caused by vacuum-enhanced light-to-heat conversion efficiency.

title: A low power flexible halide perovskite-based threshold switching memristor as an artificial nociceptor
abstract: In this study, we introduce a flexible halide perovskite based-threshold switching memristor, which exhibits outstanding low power consumption and excellent stability, as an artificial nociceptor that mimics various advanced nociceptor behaviors.

title: Construction of nanorod-shaped TiO 2 /Cu 3 N p–n heterojunction for efficient visible-light hydrogen evolution
abstract: p-Type Cu 3 N was deposited onto n-type TiO 2 nanorod arrays to form p–n heterojunction and achieve an efficient visible-light hydrogen evolution.

title: Enhanced photomultiplication in filter-free organic photodetectors for red and NIR light sensing using minimal nonfullerene blends
abstract: High sensitivity, filter-free, both red and NIR light bandpass photomultiplication OPDs are developed by the charge injection narrowing, using a minimal amount of Y6. The proposed OPDs have the potential application of cold metal detection.

title: A facile membraneless method for detecting alkali-metal cations using organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: A facile membraneless method for detecting alkali-metal cations was developed by adding ionophores (crown ethers: 18C6 or 15C5) to a polymer matrix (p(g2T-TT)) as the active layer in organic electrochemical transistors.

title: Enhancing the stability of zinc-ion batteries with titanium-doped VO 2 cathode materials
abstract: Ti–VO 2 is prepared by a hydrothermal method and used as a cathode material for zinc-ion batteries exhibiting enhanced diffusion kinetics, high capacity and excellent cycling stability.

title: Highly sensitive temperature sensing of Na(Y 1.5 Na 0.5 )F 6 glass-ceramics based on Dy 3+ /Pr 3+ energy transfer
abstract: In this paper, we synthesized a glass-ceramic luminescent material that exhibits exceptional luminescent properties and high sensitivity. It can be utilized in the field of temperature-sensing materials due to its outstanding performance.

title: A comprehensive investigation into thermoelectric properties of PEDOT:PSS/Bi 0.5 Sb 1.5 Te 3 composites
abstract: Methodologies for enhancing the conversion efficiency of organic/inorganic thermoelectric composites, enabling future applications in self-powered wearable electronic devices.

title: Theoretical investigation of width effects in the electronic and transport properties of carbon nanoribbons with 5-8-5 carbon rings: a first-principles study
abstract: An increase in width enhances stability and acts like uniaxial tensile strain. Sub-bandgap regions trigger optoelectronic device applications and negative differential resistance. Nanodevice behavior depends on the width.

title: Templated synthesis of CsPbBr 3 nanowire arrays toward low dark current drift and stable X-ray detectors
abstract: AAO–CsPbBr 3 composite films are prepared using a melt hot-pressing method, in which AAO templates are completely filled with CsPbBr 3 nanowires. X-ray detectors with excellent electrical stability are fabricated based on AAO–CsPbBr 3 films.

title: Ring-size dependent ratiometric photoluminescence of cyclophane mechanophores
abstract: Ring size determines the mechanochromic luminescence properties of cyclophane mechanophores whose mechanism relies on the dissociation of the CT complexes between a 9,10-bis(phenylethynyl)anthracene and a pyromellitic diimide.

title: Tailoring thermal expansion and luminescence thermometric performance of KMgScW 3 O 12 -based compounds
abstract: Negative thermal expansion compounds provide an opportunity to understand the nature of thermal expansion and engineering applications of inorganic solid functional materials. This work gives a suitable case to investigate the relationship between thermal expansion and luminescence thermal behavior.

title: Substituent tailoring anti-aromaticity and charge transport polarity of cyclopenta[ hi ]aceanthrylenes
abstract: Substituents at peripheral cyclopenta-fused polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons are important to regulate the anti-aromaticity, energy levels and charge transport polarity.

title: Highly efficient and stable Cs 4 PbBr 6 @KBr color conversion films constructed through inkjet printing technology
abstract: In situ crystallized Cs 4 PbBr 6 @KBr color conversion films have been obtained, showing an average pixel diameter of 16.7 μm, an impressive PLQY of 94%, and excellent stability against thermal and light irradiation.

title: Near-unity photoluminescence quantum yield in zero-dimensional lead-free indium-based hybrid perovskites by antimony doping
abstract: High-performance broadband emission with a near-unity photoluminescence quantum yield of ∼99.3% was observed in (C 4 H 12 N) 2 InC 5 ·DMF:Sb 3+ hybrid perovskites, which can be attributed to the self-trapped excitons of the [SbCl 5 ·DMF] 2− polyhedron.

title: Two-dimensional Cr 2 GaX 4 (X = S, Se, Te) monolayers: half-metallic intrinsic room temperature ferromagnets with large magnetic anisotropy
abstract: Based on first-principles calculations, we predict a series of Cr 2 GaX 4 (X = S, Se, Te) monolayers, which exhibit intrinsic ferromagnetic ordering, large magnetic anisotropy energy, and high Curie temperatures above room temperature.

title: A polarized near-infrared organic phototransistor based on a narrow-band SnPc single crystal
abstract: We fabricated a fast and broadband organic photodetector with NIR response and polarization-sensitivity based on a narrowband SnPc single crystal. Its NIR response can reach up to 38.5 A W −1 at 850 nm, with a fast response speed of 440/590 μs.

title: Polyionic liquid ionogels formed via hydrophobic association for flexible strain sensors
abstract: A series of self-healing polyionic liquid ionogels based on hydrophobic association were designed and fabricated for application in strain sensors.

title: Controllable-assembled functional monolayers by the Langmuir–Blodgett technique for optoelectronic applications
abstract: Systematic review of the application of controllable assembled LB films in combination with optoelectronic applications.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic degradation of tetracycline hydrochloride by hollow nanofiber Ag@ZnGa 2 O 4 /ZnO with synergistic effects of LSPR and S-scheme interface engineering
abstract: Compared with traditional photocatalytic materials, hollow nanofibers can show greatly improved photocatalytic efficiency due to their large specific surface area and more surface-active sites.

title: Novel valleytronic and piezoelectric properties coexisting in Janus MoAZ 3 H (A = Si, or Ge; Z = N, P, or As) monolayers
abstract: By first-principles calculations, we find that valleytronic and piezoelectric properties coexist in Janus MoAZ 3 H ML materials, providing wide applications in the fields of valleytronics and energy conversion for MoAZ 3 H MLs.

title: Dissecting the nature and dynamics of electronic excitations in a solid-state aggregate of a representative non-fullerene acceptor
abstract: Understanding electronic excitations and their dynamics in non-fullerene acceptors is crucial for enhancing opto-electronic properties. Using a Frenkel-exciton Hamiltonian and non-adiabatic dynamics, we reveal design strategies to achieve this goal.

title: A multidimensional anti-counterfeiting dynamic flexible label based on light/thermal responsive CLC microspheres
abstract: Compared to static anti-counterfeiting, polymorphic dynamic anti-counterfeiting typically incorporates additional parameters specifically to increase the complexity of decryption.

title: Windmill-type molecules for efficient deep-blue organic light-emitting diodes via hybridized local and charge-transfer excited state
abstract: High steric-hindrance windmill-type molecules exhibiting deep-blue light and large horizontal dipole ratio are designed. An efficient deep-blue device shows a maximum external quantum efficiency of 7.6% and a full width at half maximum of 55 nm.

title: Local structure effects of carbon-doping on the phase change material Ge 2 Sb 2 Te 5
abstract: In situ X-ray absorption spectroscopy on C-doped Ge 2 Sb 2 Te 5 thin films reveals that C-content increases the rigidity of the Ge–Te bond in the amorphous phase, but curiously, only at moderate doping levels.

title: Solution-processed dendrimer-based bis-tridentate iridium( iii ) complexes with red, green, and blue phosphorescence for white OLEDs
abstract: Blue, green, and red emitting solution-processable first-generation dendrimers featuring bis-tridentate iridium( iii ) complex cores are blended in a single emissive layer to provide organic light-emitting diodes that emit a warm white light.

title: A generic approach for aligning liquid crystals using solution-processed 2D materials on ITO-free surfaces
abstract: A generic strategy of employing EPD-hBN layers on non-ITO substrates (Al-doped ZnO) to achieve large area unidirectional alignment of calamitic, bent-core, and dual-frequency liquid crystals.

title: Optimized responsivity of a phototransistor using graphene oxide-doped solution-processed indium oxide active layer toward neuromorphic applications
abstract: Graphene oxide embedded in the active layer of a metal oxide transistor generates a photocurrent and also traps minority charge carriers. This dual role results in sensing and memory in a single device, leading to neuromorphic behavior.

title: Magnetic structure and properties of the compositionally complex perovskite (Y 0.2 La 0.2 Pr 0.2 Nd 0.2 Tb 0.2 )MnO 3
abstract: The magnetic structure and properties were measured in a compositionally complex perovskite manganite possessing local spin disorder on the A-site and found to be similar to an undisordered control.

title: Purely organic near-infrared afterglow systems based on a triplet excimer donor
abstract: A kind of NIR afterglow with a lifetime of 0.16 s at 808 nm was prepared using triplet excimer (PVB@PVA) as the donor. PYB 2 @PVA containing more excimers is beneficial to improve luminescence of Cy7 acceptor via a triplet-to-singlet energy transfer.

title: Ferromagnetism above room temperature in Janus Fe 2 X (X = S, Se) monolayers
abstract: Novel ferromagnetic Janus Fe 2 X (X = S, Se) monolayers exhibit a high Curie temperature associated with the itinerant-electron-mediated double-exchange and RKKY interaction.

title: Reverse intersystem crossing from high-level triplet excited electronic states of fluorescent organic molecules
abstract: Triplet-sensitized “delayed fluorescence” from DPOTAB via efficient T 4 → S 1 “hot-RISC” (hRISC) is reported, in which a suppressed population of long-lived T 1 inhibits triplet–triplet annihilation (TTA) and phosphorescence.

title: Highly tunable multilevel resistive states in VO 2 /CuInP 2 S 6 heterojunctions combining ionic migration and metal–insulator transition
abstract: CuInP 2 S 6 (CIPS) with its ionic conductivity has attracted increasing attention, due to its great potential in neuromorphic computing and smart memristor devices.

title: A long-term stable zero-thermal-quenching blue-emitting phosphor for sustainable and human-centric lighting
abstract: In this work, we have successfully synthesized a long-term stable and highly efficient blue-emitting SMAO:Eu 2+ phosphor with a double luminescence center, which does not significantly disrupt melatonin production in human-centric WLED lighting.

title: Design of chemosensors and dynamic anticounterfeiting inks based on colloidal nanoprecipitated polymers
abstract: Fluorescent polymer nanoparticles, prepared by physical incorporation of fluorescein during the nanoprecipitation of the polymers, showed applications in high-level anticounterfeiting and chemical sensing of pH, CO 2 , and acidic and basic vapors.

title: Single-molecule non-volatile memories: an overview and future perspectives
abstract: A single-molecule non-volatile memory is a crucial component of future nanoscale information storage. This article provides an overview of the design, mechanism and prospects of single-molecule non-volatile memories.

title: p-Type β-Ga 2 O 3 film room-temperature NH 3 gas sensors with fast gas sensing and a low limit of detection
abstract: p-Type β-Ga 2 O 3 films room-temperature NH 3 sensors possess fast gas sensing and low limit of detection due to the fast exothermic reactions between NH 3 gas and surface hole accumulation layer.

title: Functionalised Al( iii ) metal organic frameworks for fluorescence sensing of nitroaromatic vapours
abstract: Mixed-ligand Al( iii ) metal–organic frameworks of the MIL-53 structural type show strong fluorescence quenching upon interaction with vapour-phase nitroaromatic compounds.

title: Binuclear iridium( iii ) complexes for efficient near-infrared light-emitting electrochemical cells with electroluminescence up to 800 nm
abstract: A novel family of binuclear iridium( iii ) complexes is presented and thoroughly investigated. The compounds display near-infrared (NIR) photoluminescence and their use as NIR emitting materials in light-emitting electrochemical cells is demonstrated.

title: Melt-preparation of organic–inorganic Mn-based halide transparent ceramic scintillators for high-resolution X-ray imaging
abstract: A large size TBP 2 MnBr 4 transparent ceramic is prepared by the melt processing method, which shows a high transmittance of >80% in the wavelength range of 350 nm to 800 nm, for realizing a high-resolution (16 lp mm −1 ) X-ray imaging.

title: Recent advances in ionic molecules applied in perovskite solar cells
abstract: A review concerning the interaction mechanisms and functions of various types of ionic molecules in perovskite solar cells in the past year, as well as suggestions on their molecular design and future studies.

title: Influence of the electronic structures of diketopyrrolopyrrole-based donor–acceptor conjugated polymers on thermoelectric performance
abstract: Understanding how the electronic structure of the polymer backbone influences charge transport can provide valuable insights for designing high-performance organic thermoelectric materials.

title: Photophysical properties and excited-state dynamics of donor–acceptor–heavy-atom molecules and their application in triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion
abstract: A novel series of donor–acceptor–heavy-atom (D–A–H) molecules that simultaneously implement both photoinduced electron transfer and heavy atom effect strategies.

title: High-performance n-type polymer field-effect transistors with exceptional stability
abstract: High-mobility n-type organic transistors that maintain performance for over 1000 minutes under bias stress pave the way for complementary organic circuits, overcoming a key obstacle in the field.

title: A Lewis-base-based antisolvent method for fabricating triple-cation perovskite solar cells
abstract: The incorporation of tetrahydrofuran into ethyl acetate enhances the power conversion efficiency of the perovskite solar cell, primarily due to an elevation in the short-circuit current.

title: Achieving color-tunable luminescence in CaF 2 :Eu phosphors for multimode anti-counterfeiting
abstract: Here, this work achieves color-tunable luminescence by adjusting the valence state of Eu to produce an RPL phenomenon, combined with PL, ML, and RL. Multimode luminescence anti-counterfeiting was realized in CaF 2 :Eu phosphors.

title: Luminescence analysis of heavily Mn 2+ -doped LaMgAl 11 O 19 phosphors: crystallographic site occupation and the formation of Mn 2+ –Mn 2+ dimers
abstract: This study elucidates the progression of the photoluminescence in LaMgAl 11 O 19 : x Mn 2+ phosphors, transitioning from isolated Mn 2+ to multisite-occupied Mn 2+ , and ultimately to Mn 2+ –Mn 2+ dimers through modulation of Mn 2+ doping concentration.

title: Translating efficient fluorescence into persistent room-temperature phosphorescence by doping bipolar fluorophores into polar polymer matrix
abstract: In the doped phosphorescent films, highly polar PAA afforded the best phosphorescence performance mainly due to the strong host–guest polar–polar interaction.

title: Recent advances in cross-linkable organic hole-transporting materials for perovskite optoelectronics
abstract: Recent progress in cross-linkable organic hole-transporting materials (HTMs) in perovskite-based solar cells (PSCs) and light-emitting diodes (PeLEDs) has been systematically summarized and discussed.

title: Ambipolar charge-trapping in self-assembled nanostructures of a supramolecular miktoarm star-shaped copolymer with a zinc phthalocyanine core
abstract: Supramolecular miktoarm star-shaped copolymers with a zinc phthalocyanine core exhibit phase-separated nanostructures that enhance ambipolar charge-trapping capabilities in the organic field-effect transistor memory devices.

title: Grain boundary effect unveiled in monolayer MoS 2 for photonic neuromorphic applications
abstract: This study explores grain boundaries (GBs) in monolayer MoS 2 , highlighting their impact on photonic memristor functions. GB-free MoS 2 shows better photodetection, while GBs, though slower in speed, enable adaptive learning and synaptic behaviors.

title: Achieving high sensing sensitivity in a Dy 3+ doped garnet phosphor toward optical thermometry
abstract: We report on a highly sensitive optical temperature sensing material based on two thermally coupled energy levels ( 4 I 15/2 and 4 F 9/2 ) of Dy 3+ .

title: Low-voltage polymer monolayer transistors for high-gain unipolar and complementary logic inverters
abstract: Using thin AlO x as dielectrics, low-voltage polymer monolayer TFTs were attained with a SS of 86 mV dec −1 . The resultant unipolar and complementary inverters exhibited high voltage gains of 251 V/V at V DD = −3 V and 841 V/V at V DD = 5 V.

title: Prediction of two-dimensional large-gap magnetic semiconductors in transition metal superhalogenides
abstract: Based on first-principles calculations, we classify the transition metal superhalogenides as feasible van der Waals two-dimensional magnetic semiconductors with diverse magnetic properties and large energy gaps.

title: Efficient sky-blue perovskite light-emitting diodes by regulating the quantum well distribution of quasi-2D perovskites by suppressing lattice distortion
abstract: Using mixed EA/PEA ligands the quantum well distribution of quasi-2D perovskite can be regulated by suppressing the octahedral distortion and the sky-blue PeLEDs obtained can achieve an external quantum efficiency of 14.14%.

title: CaY 2 ZrScAl 3 O 12 :Cr 3+ —an efficient and thermally stable garnet phosphor for high-performance NIR LEDs
abstract: The optimized CYZS:0.08Cr 3+ phosphor, peaking around ∼780 nm, exhibited remarkable QE values, with IQE and EQE reaching 78% and 32% respectively.

title: A tunable light scattering device fabricated using pseudopeptide polymer incorporated chiral nematic liquid crystal
abstract: PFLC-based tunable light scattering device is fabricated using chiral nematic LC and pseudopeptide polymer.

title: Type-II WS 2 /AsP van der Waals heterojunctions with high rectification ratio and high detectivity
abstract: The WS 2 /AsP vdWHs device with a type-II band structure suppresses dark currents and improves photoelectric characteristics. It boasts a detectivity of 1.72 × 10 13 jones and a responsivity of 14.6 A W −1 , showing great potential in future applications.

title: Structural and physical properties of two distinct 2D lead halides with intercalated Cu( ii )
abstract: [Cu(O 2 C–CH 2 –NH 2 ) 2 ]Pb 2 Br 4 is a new semiconductor with a novel 2D layered crystal structure featuring intercalated Cu( ii ) in between the inorganic layers. [Cu(O 2 C–CH 2 –NH 2 ) 2 ]Pb 2 Br 4 is a candidate for semiconductor X-ray detector applications.

title: Tunable electronic structures of Janus In 2 Ge 2 X 3 Y 3 (X, Y = S, Se and Te) monolayers under external fields
abstract: Varying X/Y elements can rationally tune the electronic properties of Janus In 2 Ge 2 X 3 Y 3 (X, Y = S, Se and Te) monolayers. Applying external strains and electric fields upon them can vary the Mulliken charge distributions and electronic band gaps.

title: A three-step change in uniaxial negative thermal expansion by switching supramolecular motion modes in a ferromagnetically coupled nickel dithiolate lattice
abstract: The wheel–axle-type supramolecule ((H 3 N + –C 2 H 4 ) 2 O)([18]crown-6) 2 forms one-dimensional chains in a crystal of honeycomb [Ni(dmit) 2 ] − structure. The uniaxial NTE can be controlled by the multi-step change of the disordered state of [18]crown-6.

title: A review on perovskite-based nanocrystals as potential electrochemiluminescence emitters: challenges and future opportunities
abstract: Extensive synthetic strategies and manipulation of perovskite-based nanocrystals not only improve the inherent optical and electrical properties but also stabilize them to enhance ECL efficiency and be used as a potential ECL luminophore material.

title: Enhancement in the linear electrical properties and thermal stability of chromite perovskites through entropy engineering
abstract: Energy band structure of LaCrO 3 -based ceramics is modified by entropy engineering to enhance the linear electrical properties and thermal stability in low-temperature thermistor applications.

title: Monolayer molecular crystal field-effect transistors: advances and perspectives
abstract: To break the limitations of traditional molecular materials in electronic devices, especially in organic field-effect transistors (OFETs), developing monolayer molecular crystals (MMCs) with the ultimate thickness is crucial.

title: High-performance multilevel nonvolatile organic field-effect transistor memory based on multilayer organic semiconductor heterostructures
abstract: A multilevel organic field-effect transistor memory based on organic heterostructures is demonstrated. Benefiting from the charge trapping of the quantum well-like heterostructures, the memory exhibited multilevel nonvolatile memory properties.

title: High performance ternary organic solar cells assisted by red fluorescent materials through improved emission lifetime and complementary short wavelength light absorption
abstract: A ternary OSC was fabricated with DCJTB as the third component material, and the PCE increased from 15.52% to 16.59% due to the effective energy transfer between DCJTB and PM6.

title: Achieving highly efficient antisolvent- and annealing-free NIR perovskite light-emitting diodes by optimizing the gradient of preheating
abstract: Johan and colleagues demonstrated antisolvent- and annealing-free PeLEDs by modulating the substrate preheating gradient. The strategy is a viable alternative to the traditional antisolvent method for promoting high-quality perovskite films.

title: A multifunctional oriented nanocrystalline Pb 0.91 La 0.06 Zr 0.8 Ti 0.2 O 3 relaxor ferroelectric thin film for chip power and thermal management
abstract: PLZT 6/80/20 thin films prepared by the sol–gel method exhibit excellent energy storage, pyroelectric and electrocaloric performances, making them suitable for power and thermal management of chips.

title: Phototuning structural color and optical switching cholesteric textures in azobenzene-doped cholesteric liquid crystals
abstract: The first integration of structure modulation and phase modulation of CLCs has been accomplished. CLCs doped with azobenzene show rapid color shifts and phase modulation dependent on different intensity light source stimuli within a few seconds.

title: Enhanced photoelectric performance in Cu–Bi double halide perovskite single crystals
abstract: The low-dimensional structures of Bi-based perovskites severely restrict their optoelectronic performances. Here, a Cu–Bi double perovskite single crystal exhibits enhanced optical absorption, increased carrier mobility, and inhibition of defect recombination compared to Bi-only perovskite.

title: Microfluidic-driven short peptide hydrogels with optical waveguiding properties
abstract: Supramolecular hydrogels of D Leu– L Phe– L Phe tripeptide were prepared employing microfluidics, resulting in fibrillar structures with optical waveguiding ability in conjunction with dyes, which enable emission modulation across spectral regions.

title: Pillararene-based rare-earth luminescent probe for two-channel simultaneous detection of ROS and ATP in living cells
abstract: A supramolecular fluorescent probe based on unique fluorescence of Eu-complex and precise recognition of pillar[5]arene is developed for two channel simultaneous detection of H 2 O 2 and ATP in living cells.

title: Organic heterojunction charge-transfer chemical sensors
abstract: Chemical sensors based on blends of organic semiconductors with molecular sensitizers show exceptional sensitivity to amines by leveraging a charge transfer reaction.

title: Organic plasmonics: PEDOT nanoparticles are getting closer to the visible range
abstract: PEDOT Nanoparticles, generated by the combined use of e-beam lithography and electropolymerization show, after secondary doping, unprecedented LSPR maximum close to the visible range.

title: An integrated screening approach for designing efficient thermally activated delayed fluorescent materials for OLEDs
abstract: A novel screening process for organic materials identifies potential TADF molecules by combining luminescence stability and efficiency, and analyzing dihedral angles between TADF donors and acceptors.

title: Unravelling the white light emission in a single-phase new stoichiometric vanadate phosphor synthesized by rapid low-temperature one-pot synthesis and its W-LED prototype with high CRI and luminous efficacy
abstract: Schematic showing the synthesis of a new stoichiometric single-phase Ba 3 V 4 O 13 , its vacancy-activated intrinsic white light emission, and the W-LED prototype with CCT ∼ 5998 K, high CRI∼86.1, and LE of ∼128.4 lm W −1 .

title: Shape-controlled synthesis of CsPbBr 3 nanorods with bright pure blue emission and high stability
abstract: CsPbBr 3 NRs synthesized at 80 °C show bright blue emission at 462 nm and exhibit a high PLQY of 62.65%. The 1 : 1 mixed anti-solvents of EAC and IPA can change the state of NR surface ligands and improve the stability of NRs.

title: Facile and fast preparation of stretchable, self-adhesive, moisturizing, antifreezing and conductive tough hydrogel for wearable strain sensors
abstract: Stretchable, self-adhesive, moisturizing, antifreezing and conductive tough hydrogels for wearable strain sensors were conveniently and quickly prepared based on the catalytic mechanism of metal ions.

title: Conductive chelating agent-treated electron transfer layers for environmentally friendly and efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: A multifaceted conductive chelating agent, sodium p -styrenesulfonate (SSS), is incorporated into SnO 2 precursor solution to improve the performance of PSCs and provide in situ suppression of lead leakage by chelating with dissociative Pb 2+ .

title: Fast, highly chromatic, electrically responsive photonic crystal inks for displays
abstract: Benefiting from the introduction of SDBS and acidified carbon nanotubes, electronic inks exhibit fast electrical response performance and high chromaticity.

title: Development of degradable networked-organic semiconductors and effects on charge carrier mobility in organic thin-film transistors
abstract: Controlling the degradation rates in degradable polymers depends on the type of crosslinker used for transient OTFTs.

title: Electromagnetic modulation of conductance and susceptance in electrical devices based on silicone oil with polypyrrole–magnetite particle composites
abstract: Electrical conductance and susceptance of electrical devices with composite liquids based on silicone oil and polypyrrole-magnetite particles are finely tuned by varying electric and magnetic fields and adjusting magnetite content.

title: Band-bowing effects in lead-free double Cs 2 AgBi x Sb 1− x Cl 6 perovskites and their anion-exchanged derivatives
abstract: Green synthesis of lead-free Cs 2 AgBi x Sb 1− x Cl 6 solid-solution double perovskites and their conversion into bromide and iodide derivatives is reported.

title: Ordered growth of Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 double perovskites on PEIE-decorated SnO 2 for efficient planar solar cells
abstract: Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 films grown on PEIE-decorated SnO 2 have a more ordered arrangement of cation occupancy with reduced defects, facilitated charge transport, and improved band alignment, thereby boosting the performance of planar solar cells.

title: Recent advances and perspectives on iron-based photocatalysts
abstract: Iron based nanomaterials/complexes/alloys/MOFs have been discussed for CO 2 reduction, N 2 fixation, pollutants degradation and OER/HER via type II, Z-scheme and S-scheme heterojunctions.

title: Correction: Perovskite single crystal SCLC measurement prediction using a machine learning model
abstract: Correction for ‘Perovskite single crystal SCLC measurement prediction using a machine learning model’ by Sarvani Jowhar Khanam et al. , J. Mater. Chem. C , 2024, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TC01987C.

title: Memristor based electronic devices towards biomedical applications
abstract: This paper systematically reviewed the preparation technologies, research progress, and typical applications of memristors in biomedical fields.

title: Improving electron mobility in MoS 2 field-effect transistors by optimizing the interface contact and enhancing the channel conductance through local structural phase transition
abstract: A structural phase incorporation strategy was proposed to improve the electron mobility of MoS 2 FETs up to 237 cm 2 V −1 s −1 .

title: Regulation of microstructure and charge transport properties of cyclopentadiene-based conjugated polymers via side-chain engineering
abstract: A series of copolymers of cyclopentadiene and DPP unit with different side chains were synthesized. The copolymer with longer linear alkyl spacer and shorter branches exhibited superior performance and the highest mobility up to 2.1 cm 2 V −1 s −1 .

title: N-terminalized titanium carbide MXene coupled with graphene oxide toward optimized temporal domain-dependent nonlinear absorption
abstract: Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene by the surface termination modulation and subsequently the graphene oxide covalent functionalization for enhanced nonlinear optical performance across spectral (532 and 800 nm) and temporal (nanosecond and femtosecond) regimes.

title: Impact of pyrene orientation on the electronic properties and stability of graphene ribbons
abstract: We report a novel synthetic route to a series of fused acene derivatives, in which linear extension of the fully conjugated core proves to be an efficient method to tune optoelectronic properties.

title: Simple fabrication of laser-induced graphene functionalized with a copper-based metal–organic framework and its application in solid-state supercapacitors
abstract: Fabrication of laser-induced graphene functionalized with a metal–organic framework (Cu-BTC). The Cu-BTC@LIG composites are used as electrodes for supercapacitors.

title: General synthesis for supported single-atom catalysts using hydroxyl nests in zeolites
abstract: A general method for synthesizing single-atom catalysts (SACs) has been developed using direct impregnation, leveraging the internal silanol structure of molecular sieves to trap metal species.

title: Advanced lead-free double perovskites/silica hybrid nanocrystals for highly stable light-emitting diodes
abstract: Cs 2 AgIn 0.98 Bi 0.02 Cl 6 @KIT-6 NCs were obtained by in situ assembly within KIT-6 mesoporous molecular sieves, and their fluorescence thermal stability was significantly improved benefiting from the protection of the KIT-6 shell layer.

title: Trap engineering through chemical doping for ultralong X-ray persistent luminescence and anti-thermal quenching in Zn 2 GeO 4
abstract: Temperature-dependent photoluminescence to understand negative thermal quenching in Zn 2 GeO 4 , and Pr 3+ doping for trap tuning and the enhanced formation of defect states leading to ultralong (>18 h) X-ray persistent luminescence.

title: Cu/Ni Keggin-based viologen complexes with water-assisted 1D proton channels for asymmetric supercapacitors
abstract: Cu/Ni Keggin viologen complexes with one-dimensional proton channels are synthesized and directly used as excellent materials for supercapacitors. The 1//AC ASC exhibits an energy density of 9.39 W h kg −1 at a power density of 6000 W kg −1 .

title: Advanced photoelectrochemical performance of inverse-opal heterostructures fabricated using hydrogenated ZnO and TiO 2
abstract: Hydrogenated ZnO inverse opals, alongside hydrogenated TiO 2 inverse opals in photonic-crystal heterostructures, demonstrate advanced visible light absorptivity and photoelectrochemical performance.

title: Visible-excitable long-afterglow material with dual-mode emission of delayed fluorescence and room temperature phosphorescence
abstract: The BA@Fluo can achieve dual-mode emission of RTP and TADF, and colorful afterglow can be achieved under temperature regulation. Particularly, BA@Fluo was characterized by fluorescence discoloration, and increased quantum yield caused by grinding.

title: Green and efficient piezocatalytic hydrogen production achieved by modifying SrBi 4 Ti 4 O 15 with CdSe
abstract: The mechanical vibration-assisted piezoelectric catalytic water splitting for hydrogen production is a promising approach to address the energy crisis.

title: A narrow pulse width and high peak power passively Q-switched laser based on the saturable-absorption characteristic of 2-layer TeSe 2
abstract: We prepared TeSe 2 saturable-absorption nanosheets. The match of band-gap energy with laser wavelength was analysed, and the TeSe 2 SA was applied in solid-state lasers to obtain narrow temporal-width and high peak pulse output.

title: A multi-stimuli responsive polyoxometalate-guanosine monophosphate hybrid chromogenic smart hydrogel
abstract: We propose facile synthesis of POM-GMP supramolecular hydrogel exhibiting chromism in response to multiple stimuli-light, electricity, temperature, stress, iodide, pH and biologically relevant thiols such as cysteine and glutathione.

title: Glue-like passivation by the natural alkene lycopene for efficient and stable perovskite solar cells: insights from a theoretical perspective
abstract: Conjugated CC bonds of alkene achieve robust interaction in cover style, which is beneficial for stability and ion migration in long-term and efficient perovskite solar cells.

title: Direct writing of flexible two-dimensional MXene arrays for SERS sensing
abstract: A simple direct writing method to on-demand design flexible 2D M 2 C (M = Ti, V, Mo, Nb) MXene SERS substrates is developed.

title: Supercapacitors with enhanced energy storage and hydrogen evolution reaction performance via sequential alkali-modified MXenes
abstract: This study explores hydrothermal-assisted sequential KOH treatment of MXenes, introducing –O and –OH functional groups. It improves surface area, reduces F-terminations, improves capacitance, cyclic stability, and enhances HER performance.

title: Polymerization-induced diffusion-driven formation of a cholesteric liquid crystal polymer network film with double helical pitches
abstract: Cholesteric liquid crystal polymer network films were prepared through polymerization-induced chiral dopant diffusion, and were suitably applied for decoration and anti-counterfeiting.

title: Ferromagnetic Dirac half-metallicity in edge-modified zigzag boron nitride nanoribbons
abstract: Fully spin polarized Dirac fermions, with tunable gaps and room temperature ferromagnetism are discovered, by passivating single and both edges of ZBNNRs.

title: A dilute ferromagnetic ZrO 2 /carbon nanocomposite derived from a zirconium-based metal–organic framework for high-performance electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Dilute ferromagnetic ZrO 2 /carbon nanocomposites have been fabricated based on a Zr-MOF. The ZrO 2 /C_700 nanocomposite exhibits a superior reflection loss of −59.69 dB at 2.88 mm and a wide effective absorption bandwidth of 6.44 GHz at 2.36 mm.

title: Fabrication of highly sensitive flexible phototransistors through a unidirectional floating film transfer method using P4T2F-HD thin films
abstract: This study introduces a fabrication technique for fabricating highly sensitive, flexible organic phototransistors (OPTs) suitable for advanced wearable and smart home applications.

title: The limiting factors of quantum-cutting efficiency of ytterbium-doped lead halide perovskites: dimeric and monomeric ytterbium species
abstract: Monomeric and dimeric Yb species form in Yb-doped cesium lead halide perovskites. At low concentration monomers create a quantum-cutting efficiency loss channel, while diffusion-limited quenching of Yb excited states dominates at high concentration.

title: Ultrahigh toughness of stretchable ratiometric mechanofluorescent polyurethane elastomers enhanced by dual slide-ring motion of polyrotaxane cross-linkers and daisy chain backbones
abstract: Mechanofluorophoric polyurethane elastomers containing dual slide-ring motion of daisy chain backbones and polyrotaxane crosslinkers are created, which show noble mechanical features and ratiometric mechanofluorescent responses under tensile forces.

title: Using spatial confinement to decipher polymorphism in the organic semiconductor p-DTS(FBTTh 2 ) 2
abstract: Spatial confinement at the nanoscale is exploited to achieve specific polymorphs in the organic semiconductor p-DTS(FBTTh 2 ) 2 . A new polymorph exhibiting higher charge-carrier mobility compared to previously reported crystal form is found.

title: Synergistic interaction of multi-functional additives at the buried interface for efficient blue perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: The synergistic effect of the doping strategy of basic amino acid salts at the interface enables the simultaneous modification of PEDOT:PSS and the bottom interface of the perovskite film to achieve efficient sky-blue PeLEDs.

title: High quality MAPbBr 3 films via pulsed laser deposition of single-crystalline targets
abstract: PLD of single-crystalline targets represents a route to grow thin films of metal halide perovskites with narrow photoluminescence peaks characteristic of high crystalline quality.

title: Additive-assisted molecular aggregation manipulation towards efficient thick organic solar cells
abstract: The additive 1-phenylnaphthalene (PN) with large steric hindrance is used to promote J-aggregation of N3 molecules, which is conductive to broadening absorption spectra and improving charge transport for high-performance thick OSCs.

title: Fine control of Ce doped CH 3 NH 3 PbBr 3 to modulate photoluminescence and carrier characteristics for application in photoconductive photodetectors
abstract: Finely tunable amounts of Ce 3+ ions into MAPbBr 3 can improve the opto-electronic properties of photodetector.

title: Temperature dependence of charge transport in molecular ensemble junctions
abstract: This study investigates the temperature dependence of conductivity in molecular junctions under various biasing regimes, providing insights into charge transport mechanisms and their impact on device functionality.

title: Advanced cobalt-based hierarchical and gradient armor catalysts for high-performance Li–S batteries over a wide temperature range
abstract: Developing efficient and long-lasting cathode catalysts is crucial for preparing high-performance lithium–sulfur (Li–S) batteries.

title: NIR-triggered logic gate in MXene-modified perovskite resistive random access memory
abstract: The NIR-responsive MXene@MAPbI 3 -based resistive switching RRAMs at 1064 nm are prepared. They can be applied in integrated data-storing and information-processing, especially in in vivo manually controlled biocompatible microchips.

title: Achieving 18.92% efficiency of non-fullerene organic solar cells with active layer morphology optimization by regulating solvent evaporation dynamics
abstract: 18.92% efficiency of D18:L8-BO organic solar cells achieved by regulating the solvent evaporation process using CF : Tol mixed solvent.

title: Progress of Ce 3+ /Eu 2+ doped phosphors for violet chip based full-spectra lighting
abstract: The research progress of Ce 3+ /Eu 2+ doped phosphors that can be excited by violet light, including the structural models, composition regulation, and spectral tuning, is summarized.

title: Multi-color afterglow of the LiGa 5 O 8 :Tb 3+ /Sm 3+ co-doped gallosilicate glass via energy transfer and trap sharing for optical anti-counterfeiting
abstract: LiGa 5 O 8 :Tb 3+ ,Sm 3+ doped glass-ceramics with a multi-color afterglow are promising for optical encryption information anti-counterfeiting and storage fields.

title: Chemical bath deposition of Sb 2 S 3 thin films using the mixing solution of SbCl 3 and sodium citrate as a novel Sb source for assembling efficient solar cells
abstract: Sb 2 S 3 films are prepared by CBD using the mixing solution of SbCl 3 and sodium citrate as a novel Sb source. The CBD growth mechanism of Sb 2 S 3 films is revealed and the CBD growth process is dominated by the nanoparticle formation–adsorption pathway.

title: Real-time and self-monitoring lubricant enabled by the triboluminescence of ZnS:Cu/GF/PTFE composites
abstract: In this work, we fabricated ZnS:Cu/GF/PTFE lubricating composites with remarkable triboluminescence, based on which the working parameters of solid lubricants and the service lifetime of lubricating oils could be self-monitored.

title: Ce 3+ -doped alkaline-earth sulfide nanocrystals for X-ray scintillation imaging screens
abstract: The uniform SrS:Ce 3+ and CaS:Ce 3+ nanocrystals provide new possibilities for large-area and fast X-ray scintillation imaging screens.

title: A high-performance organic–inorganic self-powered broadband photodetector based on PANI/Bi 2 O 2 S nanocomposites
abstract: A high-performance organic–inorganic self-powered broadband photodetector based on PANI/Bi 2 O 2 S nanocomposites exhibits ultrahigh responsiveness and fast response time.

title: Enhanced thermal conductivity of polymeric composites with BN@C hybrid fillers
abstract: The BN@carbon particle hybrid fillers were designed to enhance the thermal conductivity of composites through bridging and separation effects.

title: LTP–LTD transformation of unipolar pulse voltage-driven zinc oxide memristors via TiO 2 thin layer incorporation
abstract: ZnO-based memristors are considered to be promising candidates in neuromorphic computing because of their nonvolatile resistive switching characteristics.

title: A design strategy for high-performance vertical organic field-effect transistors based on reduced graphene oxide electrodes
abstract: Through analysis and rational design of the device, a high-performance rGO-VOFETs was achieved with a maximum current density of 134.5 mA cm −2 .

title: A novel zero-dimensional organic–inorganic hybrid ferroelectric material
abstract: In recent years, organic–inorganic hybrid ferroelectric materials with zero-dimensional (0D) structures have attracted widespread attention due to their fascinating characteristics arising from isolated metal halide structures.

title: A review on recent research progress in perovskite-based gas sensors
abstract: Standards for high-performance perovskite gas sensors.

title: Heavy metal-free colloidal quantum dots: preparation and application in infrared photodetectors
abstract: Heavy metal-free colloidal quantum dots (CQDs) are ideal materials for overcoming heavy metal problems. In this paper, the synthesis progress of CQDs and their phased research progress in the field of multi-type infrared detection are reviewed.

title: Relaxor induced performance tuning around morphotropic phase boundary in Ba 0.86 Sr 0.14 Ti 0.94 Sn 0.06 modified BNT-based ceramics
abstract: Optimized piezoelectric property and strain as well as improved stability based on a nonergodic to ergodic relaxor with reduced depolarization temperature in sodium bismuth titanate with a morphotropic phase boundary.

title: Open system massive synthesis of narrow-band blue and green fluorescent graphene quantum dots and their application in water sensing
abstract: PG-derived graphene quantum dots (GQDs) were obtained with 99.4% product yield and exhibited narrow-band blue fluorescence with a high quantum yield.

title: Trade-off between processability and device performance in donor–acceptor semiconductors revealed using discrete siloxane side chains
authors: Bart W. L. van den Bersselaar
abstract: The fine balance between enhanced processability and decreased device performance in donor–acceptor polymeric semiconductors is elucidated using discrete siloxane side chains.

title: Well-defined polymorph transformation sequences and novel tetragonal twin-domain patterns in the morphotropic phase boundary of Dy-modified BiFeO 3 –PbTiO 3 single crystals
abstract: Well-defined polymorph transformation sequences and novel tetragonal twin-domain patterns are found in the MPB of Dy-modified BiFeO 3 –PbTiO 3 single crystals.

title: Enhanced near-infrared phosphorescence found in a structurally similar host–guest system
abstract: Enhanced NIR phosphorescence emission in the longer-wavelength region can be produced from a highly ordered host–guest aggregate structure arising from the same crystal packing pattern of the host and guest molecules with structural similarity.

title: Enhanced nanogenerator by embedding lead-free double perovskite Cs 2 AgBiBr 6 in polymer matrix for hybrid energy harvesting
abstract: CABB addition with high dielectric constant and low dielectric loss facilitates PVDF phase transition and device outputs enhancement.

title: Synthesis of phosphorescent syn , anti -isomeric clamshell platinum( ii ) dimers for OLED applications
abstract: Six clamshell diplatinum( ii ) complexes with a formula of [Pt(TBPCH)(N^N)] 2 bearing both a carbazole-pyrimidine chelate and a N^N bridge, (TBPCH 2 = 3,6-di- tert -butyl-9-(pyrimidin-2-yl)-9 H -carbazole), have been prepared by utilizing TBPCH 2 (C^N) as the primary ligand and N^N as the bridging l…

title: Superconducting Ce 2 P 3 and CeP 2 with an interesting planar P layer
abstract: Ce 2 P 3 with 3-4-5-polygon P layer and CeP 2 with honeycomb P layer exhibit an enhanced superconductivity with decreasing pressure.

title: Unveiling the significance of adduct formation between thiocarbonyl Lewis donors and diiodine for the structural organization of rhodanine-based small molecule semiconductors
abstract: Rhodanine vinyl bithiophene was reacted with molecular iodine to give a 1 : 1 stoichiometric charge-transfer adduct whose crystals, structurally and spectroscopically characterized, exhibited remarkable stability and enhanced conductivity.

title: Adjustable 1D ferroelectricity and ferrielectricity in faceted GeSe nanotubes
abstract: We proposed a set of faceted GeSe nanotubes (GNTs) which can exist in carbon nanotubes. 1D adjustable ferroelectricity and ferrielectricity were found in the single- and double-walled GNTs.

title: Ionic liquid-regulated PbI 2 layers and defect passivation for efficient perovskite solar cells
abstract: In this work, formamidine acetate (FAAc) was introduced in the two-step method to regulate perovskite crystallization and passivate defects, which resulted in achieving the PCE of device exceeding 24% with notable improvements in stability.

title: Next-generation wearable sensors: toward multi-directional strain sensing in sensory integration platforms
abstract: Strain sensors capable of recognizing the direction of mechanical stimuli are a key contributor to the development of wearable sensory platforms.

title: New pyrochlore La 2 Zr 2 O 7 ceramics with ultra-high breakdown electric field strength and energy storage efficiency
abstract: The application potential of the La 2 Zr 2 O 7 ceramic as a lead-free dielectric capacitor was first proposed and investigated. The breakdown field strength is 1350 kV cm −1 , the energy storage density is 3.89 J cm −3 , and the efficiency is 89.78%.

title: Alkali metal-doped two-dimensional Janus Cr 2 Br 3 I 3 monolayers with the quantum anomalous Hall effect
abstract: The Li/Na/K-doped Cr 2 Br 3 I 3 monolayer comprehensively provides an “example” of the study of QAHE, i.e. , a structure in which the electronic structure shows whether QAHE can be realized in three examples: “Yes”, “Needs to be fine-tuned”, and “No”.

title: Enhanced quantum efficiency and thermal stability by crystal-field engineering in a Y(Ga,Al) 3 (BO 3 ) 4 :Cr 3+ ,Yb 3+ phosphor for diverse short-wave infrared applications
abstract: In this work, a novel NIR phosphor YGa 1.8 Al 1.2 (BO 3 ) 4 :Cr 3+ was synthesized. By designing the Cr 3+ –Yb 3+ energy transfer, efficient SWIR luminescence with simultaneously high IQE, thermal stability and output power was achieved.

title: Delafossite CuGaO 2 nanomaterial-based room temperature H 2 S selective gas sensor
abstract: To minimize harmful gas exposure and enable early disease diagnoses in low-resource settings, it is crucial to create an H 2 S gas sensor that can detect low ppb levels at room temperature (RT).

title: Unveiling symmetry: a comparative analysis of asymmetric and symmetric non-fullerene acceptors in organic solar cells
abstract: This study investigates the design and analysis of symmetric and asymmetric non-fullerene acceptors (NFAs), focusing on the burgeoning interest in asymmetric NFAs due to their exceptional solar cell properties.

title: Light-responsive smart nanopapers and ink: design for information storage and encryption
abstract: In the present study, spiropyran (SP)-like photoactive molecules were embedded in situ into cavities of lanthanide metal–organic frameworks (Ln-MOFs), resulting in individually aligned molecules to generate dynamically photo-responsive materials (Ln-MOFs@SP).

title: Pb 2 Ga 3 F 6 (SeO 3 ) 2 X 3 ·2H 2 O (X = Cl, Br): two new HTO-type members exhibiting large NLO effects mediated by ionic mixing and substitution strategies
abstract: Pb 2 Ga 3 F 6 (SeO 3 ) 2 X 3 ·2H 2 O achieve a better balance between the large SHG effect and wide band gap in the current HTO family.

title: Configuration-dependent hollow heterostructures for highly efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: A configuration-dependent strategy significantly boosts the photocatalytic hydrogen production efficiency of hollow core–shell heterostructures through improved light response range, carrier transport, and electron–hole separation rates.

title: The isomerization effect of a benzothiophene-substituted benzodithiophene-based donor polymer on the blend morphology and photovoltaic performance of all-polymer solar cells
abstract: Two polymers of PBS-DIT and PTPH-DIT were designed and synthesized, and the influence of isomerization on the blend morphology and device performance was investigated.

title: New ultrafast scintillators with core valence luminescence: Cs 2 MgCl 4 and Cs 3 MgCl 5
abstract: Single crystals of Cs 2 MgCl 4 and Cs 3 MgCl 5 are grown by the Bridgman method and revealed as promising scintillator materials.

title: Modulating the crystal packing to achieve efficient ultralong organic phosphorescence by simple methylation engineering
